what: Closed-loop Autonomous Active Learning for soil mapping — al_loop
title: Closed-loop Autonomous Active Learning for soil mapping — al_loop
text: Runs full Pillar 5 closed loop: fit Quantile Regression Forest current labeled set; query next batch candidates via al_query(); label user-supplied oracle (simulation oracle, reference values already candidates); append + refit; repeat sampling budget exhausted OOB RMSE falls stop_rmse.

title: Closed-loop Autonomous Active Learning for soil mapping — al_loop
code: al_loop(   labeled,   candidates,   target,   covariates,   coords = NULL,   budget = 30L,   batch = 5L,   strategy = c("hybrid", "uncertainty", "diverse", "cost"),   alpha = 0.7,   quantiles = c(0.1, 0.9),   base = NULL,   cost_weight = 0.3,   physics_gate = NULL,   oracle = NULL,   stop_rmse = NULL,   num.trees = 500L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Closed-loop Autonomous Active Learning for soil mapping — al_loop
text: labeled Data frame initial labeled sample. candidates Data frame unlabeled candidate locations. oracle NULL, candidates[[target]] must already contain true values (simulation mode). target Character, name target column. covariates Character vector covariate column names. coords Optional length-2 character vector naming x/y columns. budget Integer, total number samples loop allowed query (addition initial set). batch Integer, number samples query per iteration. strategy Query strategy — see al_query(). alpha, quantiles, base, cost_weight, physics_gate Forwarded al_query(). See al_physics_gate_piml() PIML-backed physics gate couples Pillar 5 loop Pillar 2 fit. oracle Optional labelling function function(samples) -> numeric. NULL, simulation oracle reads true target candidates. stop_rmse Optional early-stop threshold OOB RMSE. num.trees Integer, forest size used every refit. verbose Logical; print per-iteration diagnostics.

title: Closed-loop Autonomous Active Learning for soil mapping — al_loop
text: edaphos_al_model whose $history contains one entry per iteration (iter 0 initial fit).

title: Closed-loop Autonomous Active Learning for soil mapping — al_loop
text: oracle argument extension point lets code drive simulation, lab-pipeline integration, -board Edge-AI decision making drone/rover (Pillar-5 endgame).

title: Closed-loop Autonomous Active Learning for soil mapping — al_loop
code: # \donttest{   if (requireNamespace("sp", quietly = TRUE)) {     data(meuse, package = "sp")     d <- stats::na.omit(meuse[, c("x", "y", "dist", "elev",                                   "ffreq", "soil", "lead")])     d$ffreq <- as.numeric(d$ffreq)     d$soil  <- as.numeric(d$soil)     set.seed(1)     seed_idx  <- al_initial_design(d, c("dist", "elev", "ffreq", "soil"),                                    n = 15, iter = 500)     m <- al_loop(       labeled    = d[seed_idx, ],       candidates = d[-seed_idx, ],       target     = "lead",       covariates = c("dist", "elev", "ffreq", "soil"),       coords     = c("x", "y"),       budget     = 20, batch = 5,       strategy   = "hybrid", verbose = FALSE     )     al_history(m)   } #>   iter n_labeled rmse_oob mean_uncertainty #> 1    0        15 53.49527               NA #> 2    1        20 63.21191            242.6 #> 3    2        25 63.28423            227.0 #> 4    3        30 62.15733            234.4 #> 5    4        35 67.83500            198.2 # }

what: Build a physics gate from a PIML profile fit — al_physics_gate_piml
title: Build a physics gate from a PIML profile fit — al_physics_gate_piml
text: Convenience helper constructing physics_gate argument al_query() al_loop() existing PIML fit — either parametric profile (piml_profile_fit()) Neural ODE variant (piml_neural_ode_fit()).

title: Build a physics gate from a PIML profile fit — al_physics_gate_piml
code: al_physics_gate_piml(   profile_fit,   safety_factor = 1.2,   lower = NULL,   upper = NULL )

title: Build a physics gate from a PIML profile fit — al_physics_gate_piml
text: profile_fit edaphos_piml_profile edaphos_piml_neural_ode object. safety_factor Numeric (>= 1). 1 = strict envelope; 1.2 = allow 20 % slack side. lower Optional hard lower bound target units (e.g. 0 mass fractions). supplied, overrides lower end envelope. upper Optional hard upper bound.

title: Build a physics gate from a PIML profile fit — al_physics_gate_piml
text: function function(candidates, predicted_mean) suitable al_query(..., physics_gate = <>).

title: Build a physics gate from a PIML profile fit — al_physics_gate_piml
text: gate rejects candidate whose model-predicted target value falls outside physically plausible envelope derived PIML fit. parametric ODE envelope [min(y0, y_inf), max(y0, y_inf)]; Neural ODE range predictions z = 0 deepest training depth (extrapolated 2 * max(depth) conservative floor). widened safety_factor.

what: Per-location physics gate backed by a hierarchical PIML fit — al_physics_gate_piml_hierarchical
title: Per-location physics gate backed by a hierarchical PIML fit — al_physics_gate_piml_hierarchical
text: Upgrades global-envelope al_physics_gate_piml() per-candidate envelope: candidate's profile predicted covariates, giving tighter physical plausibility window driven local pedogenic context.

title: Per-location physics gate backed by a hierarchical PIML fit — al_physics_gate_piml_hierarchical
code: al_physics_gate_piml_hierarchical(   hier_fit,   candidate_covariate_cols = hier_fit$covariate_cols,   safety_factor = 1.2,   envelope_depths = c(0, 5, 15, 30, 60) )

title: Per-location physics gate backed by a hierarchical PIML fit — al_physics_gate_piml_hierarchical
text: hier_fit edaphos_piml_hierarchical. candidate_covariate_cols Character vector column names candidate table correspond training covariates (must set, can different order — function reorders ). safety_factor Numeric >= 1, widening factor side. envelope_depths Numeric, depths (units training) profile probed compute min max candidate. Defaults span surface deep horizon.

title: Per-location physics gate backed by a hierarchical PIML fit — al_physics_gate_piml_hierarchical
text: function suitable al_query(..., physics_gate = <>).

what: Query the most informative unlabeled candidates — al_query
title: Query the most informative unlabeled candidates — al_query
text: Given fitted Active-Learning model, picks next n candidates sample according trade-strategy. strategies use greedy batch-mode selection: second later picks within call see previously picked points already labeled, batch remains internally diverse.

title: Query the most informative unlabeled candidates — al_query
code: al_query(   model,   candidates,   n = 5L,   strategy = c("hybrid", "uncertainty", "diverse", "cost"),   alpha = 0.7,   quantiles = c(0.1, 0.9),   base = NULL,   cost_weight = 0.3,   physics_gate = NULL )

title: Query the most informative unlabeled candidates — al_query
text: model edaphos_al_model. candidates Data frame unlabeled candidate locations. Must contain model$covariates. Rows NA covariate ignored. n Integer, batch size. strategy One "hybrid", "uncertainty", "diverse", "cost". alpha Numeric [0, 1], weight uncertainty vs diversity hybrid / cost. quantiles Length-2 numeric lower/upper probabilities used measure QRF interval width. Defaults c(0.1, 0.9). base Numeric length-2 vector c(x, y); required strategy = "cost". cost_weight Numeric, weight cost penalty "cost". physics_gate Optional function function(candidates, predicted_mean) -> logical returns TRUE physically feasible candidates FALSE infeasible ones. Infeasible rows excluded greedy selection, linking Pillar 5 Pillar 2 — see al_physics_gate_piml() ready-made gate driven PIML profile fit.

title: Query the most informative unlabeled candidates — al_query
text: Integer vector row indices candidates form next batch, selection order.

title: Query the most informative unlabeled candidates — al_query
text: "uncertainty" — highest QRF prediction-interval width (pure exploitation model uncertainty). "diverse" — max-min distance standardised covariate space current labeled set already-picked batch members (pure exploration). "hybrid" — convex combination alpha * uncertainty + (1 - alpha) * diversity 0-1 scaled scores. recommended default. "cost" — "hybrid" minus cost_weight * cost, cost 0-1 normalised Euclidean distance logistical base (x, y). Use steer autonomous sampler (drone, rover) towards points can physically reach limited energy.

title: BatchBALD information-theoretic batch acquisition — al_query_batchbald
text: Selects information-theoretically optimal batch Active Learning queries pool candidates, following Kirsch, van Amersfoort Gal 2019 (see @references section ). Unlike top-n BALD strategy (repeatedly picks single uncertain candidate therefore tends select n copies "question" clustered pools), BatchBALD optimises joint mutual information batch \(y_B = (y_{x_1}, \ldots, y_{x_n})\) model parameters:

title: BatchBALD information-theoretic batch acquisition — al_query_batchbald
code: al_query_batchbald(   model,   candidates,   n = 5L,   sigma_a2 = NULL,   physics_gate = NULL )

title: BatchBALD information-theoretic batch acquisition — al_query_batchbald
text: model edaphos_al_model al_fit() al_loop(). underlying ranger::ranger object must trained keep.inbag = TRUE (default al_fit()), per-tree predictions available via predict(..., predict.= TRUE). candidates Data frame unlabelled candidates. Must contain covariates listed model$covariates. n Integer — batch size. sigma_a2 Optional numeric — aleatoric noise variance \(\sigma_a^2\). NULL (default), estimated --bag residuals fitted forest. physics_gate Optional function function(candidates, predicted_mean) -> logical. See al_query().

title: BatchBALD information-theoretic batch acquisition — al_query_batchbald
text: Integer vector row indices candidates form selected batch, greedy-selection order (first index highest-BALD single point; subsequent index point maximally increases joint log-determinant given previously selected batch).

title: BatchBALD information-theoretic batch acquisition — al_query_batchbald
text: $$   \mathrm{BatchBALD}(B) \;=\;     \bigl(y_B ; \theta \mid x_B, \mathcal D\bigr). $$ regression model Gaussian aleatoric noise variance \(\sigma_a^2\) epistemic posterior represented T parameter draws \(f_\theta^{(1)}, \ldots, f_\theta^{(T)}\), objective reduces log-determinant : $$   \mathrm{BatchBALD}(B) \;\propto\;   \tfrac{1}{2}\log\det\!\bigl(     \mathrm{Cov}_\theta\bigl(f_\theta(B)\bigr) + \sigma_a^2 I_{|B|}   \bigr). $$ Quantile Regression Forest (al_fit() produces) trees T parameter draws, joint covariance just per-tree empirical covariance across candidates. greedy selection inherits \((1 - 1/e)\)-optimality guarantee submodular maximisation (Nemhauser, Wolsey Fisher 1978) implemented via Schur-complement / Cholesky updates every greedy step \(O(m^2 n_\mathrm{pool})\) rather \(O(m^3 n_\mathrm{pool})\). complement al_query(), replacement: hybrid uncertainty + diversity strategy remains default low-budget settings physical-distance term needed. Use BatchBALD () covariate pool contains clusters near-duplicate candidates top-n BALD select , (b) QRF aleatoric noise well-estimated, (c) batch size moderate (n <= 50 laptop-scale pools ~10 000 candidates).

title: BatchBALD information-theoretic batch acquisition — al_query_batchbald
text: Kirsch, ., van Amersfoort, J. Gal, Y. (2019). BatchBALD: Efficient diverse batch acquisition deep Bayesian active learning. NeurIPS 32, 7024–7035. Meinshausen, N. (2006). Quantile regression forests. Journal Machine Learning Research 7, 983–999. Nemhauser, G. L., Wolsey, L. . Fisher, M. L. (1978). analysis approximations maximizing submodular set functions — . Mathematical Programming 14, 265–294.

title: BatchBALD information-theoretic batch acquisition — al_query_batchbald
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   al <- al_initial_design(br_cerrado, c("elev","slope","twi"),                            n = 20L, seed = 1L)   fit <- al_fit(al, target = "soc")   pool <- br_cerrado[setdiff(seq_len(nrow(br_cerrado)), al$idx), ]   batch <- al_query_batchbald(fit, pool, n = 10L) } # }

title: Causal Active Learning: query the next sample(s) that most reduce the uncertainty of a targeted causal effect — al_query_causal
text: Closes loop Pillar 1 (causal identification via backdoor adjustment) Pillar 5 (autonomous active learning): instead choosing candidates marginal-Y uncertainty (classical AL), choose expected shrinkage Var(beta_hat_{X->Y}).

text: data Data frame exposure, outcome adjustment columns (current labelled set). pool Data frame columns data; unlabelled candidate pool query. dag dagitty DAG. exposure, outcome Character; column names causal query. adjustment Optional character vector adjustment-set columns.  Inferred dag via causal_adjustment_set() NULL. n_select Integer; many candidates return.  Default 5L. strategy One "leverage" (hat-matrix diagonal, closed-form, fast) "bootstrap" (re-bootstrap effect candidate; slow exact linear-OLS estimator). B Bootstrap replications strategy = "bootstrap". seed RNG seed bootstrap reproducibility.

what: Append newly labeled samples and refit the model — al_update
title: Append newly labeled samples and refit the model — al_update

title: Real-data Cerrado pedogenetic DAG — causal_cerrado_real_dag
text: Structural causal model exact covariate column names appear v1.3.1 case-study bundle (WoSIS 0-10 cm topsoil SOC + SoilGrids + WorldClim + SRTM + WorldCover). Unlike causal_cerrado_dag(), uses short schematic labels (elev, slope, twi, map_mm, ndvi, soc), DAG wired bio1, bio12, soilgrids_clay, wc_landcover_trees etc. causal_estimate_effect() can consume real profiles data frame without renaming.

text: dagitty DAG whose nodes match column names profiles data frame inst/extdata/case_cerrado_results.rds.

title: Real-data Cerrado pedogenetic DAG — causal_cerrado_real_dag
text: edges encode six classes Cerrado pedogenetic relations: Relief -> climate Elevation modulates temperature (adiabatic lapse) precipitation (orographic forcing) via elev -> bio1, elev -> bio12, elev -> slope. Climate -> vegetation / land cover Bio1 (mean annual temperature) bio12 (mean annual precipitation) drive fraction land covered trees, grassland cropland. Relief -> texture Steep slopes export fine fractions (slope -> soilgrids_clay) accumulate coarse fractions downslope (slope -> soilgrids_sand). Texture -> bulk density Fine-textured soils compact differently (soilgrids_clay -> soilgrids_bdod). Climate + texture -> SOC (direct) sides drive decomposition vs mineral protection. Land cover -> SOC Native savanna vs. pasture vs. cropland produce 3-4x SOC differences Cerrado topsoil; land-cover fractions dominant single factor.

text: estimator = "lm" — closed-form linear regression \(Y = \beta_0 + \beta_{\text{exposure}}\,X +             \sum_{z\Z}\gamma_z z + \varepsilon\). regression coefficient exposure direct causal effect. Confidence intervals follow OLS asymptotics. estimator = "bart" — non-linear Bayesian Additive Regression Trees (Chipman, George & McCulloch 2010), via dbarts Suggests dependency. effect exposure computed average partial derivative \(\bar{\partial} = \frac{1}{n}\sum_i        \bigl[\widehat{E}[Y\mid X=x_i+\delta, Z=z_i]             - \widehat{E}[Y\mid X=x_i, Z=z_i]\bigr] / \delta\) averaged training data. 95 % credible interval recovered BART posterior draws.

title: Estimate a causal effect using DAG-guided backdoor adjustment — causal_estimate_effect
text: Chipman, H. ., George, E. ., & McCulloch, R. E. (2010). BART: Bayesian Additive Regression Trees. Annals Applied Statistics 4, 266-298.

title: Two-stage least squares (2SLS) instrumental variable estimator — causal_iv_fit_2sls
text: Estimates causal effect endogenous exposure exposure outcome outcome using one instruments, optionally conditional exogenous controls.  Implements classical 2SLS estimator Wooldridge (2010) asymptotic variance – naive lm() SE second stage biased treats generated regressor observed, common mistake.

title: Two-stage least squares (2SLS) instrumental variable estimator — causal_iv_fit_2sls

title: Two-stage least squares (2SLS) instrumental variable estimator — causal_iv_fit_2sls

title: Two-stage least squares (2SLS) instrumental variable estimator — causal_iv_fit_2sls
text: edaphos_causal_iv object components effect, se, ci, stage1_F, stage1_R2, sargan_p (NULL exactly identified), n, plus auxiliary fits inspection.

title: Two-stage least squares (2SLS) instrumental variable estimator — causal_iv_fit_2sls
text: Let X endogenous exposure (one column), Z matrix instruments, W matrix exogenous controls (included stages). Define augmented matrices X_all = [W, X] Z_all = [W, Z] projection P = Z_all (Z_all' Z_all)^-1 Z_all'.  2SLS estimator $$\hat{\beta} = (X_{\text{}}^\top P X_{\text{}})^{-1} X_{\text{}}^\top P Y$$ residuals computed using ORIGINAL X_all, first-stage fitted values, $$\hat{\sigma}^2 = (Y - X_{\text{}} \hat{\beta})^\top (Y - X_{\text{}} \hat{\beta}) / (n - k)$$ $$\widehat{\mathrm{Var}}(\hat{\beta}) = \hat{\sigma}^2 (X_{\text{}}^\top P X_{\text{}})^{-1}$$ Identification conditions (Wooldridge 2010, section 5.1): Relevance: \(\mathrm{rank}(Z' X) = \dim(X)\); first-stage F-statistic exceed 10 (Stock & Yogo 2005). Exclusion: \(Z\) affects \(Y\) \(X\). Unconfoundedness: \(Z \perp U\) \(U\) unobserved confounder.

title: Sargan test for instrument over-identification — causal_iv_sargan_test
text: $$J = n \cdot R^2_{uu}$$ R^2_uu R-squared regressing 2SLS residuals instruments (controls).  H0, J ~ chi-sq(L - K) L number instruments K number endogenous regressors.  Rejection (p < 0.05) evidence least one instrument invalid.

title: Export a Knowledge Graph to RDF 1.1 Turtle — causal_kg_to_turtle
code: causal_kg_to_turtle(   kg,   path = NULL,   base_uri = "https://edaphos.io/kg/",   schema_uri = "https://edaphos.io/schema#",   namespaces = character(0),   include_metadata = TRUE )

title: Export a Knowledge Graph to RDF 1.1 Turtle — causal_kg_to_turtle
text: kg edaphos_causal_kg. path Optional output file path. NULL (default), Turtle document returned single character string. base_uri Character — base IRI KG. Must end "/" "#". Default "https://edaphos.io/kg/". schema_uri Character — IRI eds: schema namespace. Default "https://edaphos.io/schema#". namespaces Named character vector extra prefix bindings declare. Useful want reference external vocabularies (e.g. c(agrovoc = "http://aims.fao.org/aos/agrovoc/")). include_metadata Logical — emit document-level metadata (creation time, edaphos version) prov: statements. Default TRUE.

title: Export a Knowledge Graph to RDF 1.1 Turtle — causal_kg_to_turtle
text: path NULL, invisibly returns Turtle document length-1 character vector. path given, writes document disk invisibly returns path.

title: Export a Knowledge Graph to RDF 1.1 Turtle — causal_kg_to_turtle
text: emitted graph uses following prefixes default (overridable via namespaces): ed:   — edaphos KG namespace (one IRI per node edge). eds:  — edaphos schema namespace (defines eds:Causes, eds:confidence, eds:evidence, eds:source). rdf:, rdfs:, xsd:, prov:, dct: — standard W3C vocabularies. Every node L becomes IRI <base_uri>node/<sanitised_L>; every edge becomes IRI <base_uri>edge/<cause>__<effect> typed rdf:Statement, rdf:subject / rdf:predicate / rdf:object pointing nodes eds:Causes used predicate causal direction. Provenance attached via dct:source (bibliographic identifier) prov:generatedAtTime (timestamp).

title: Export a Knowledge Graph to RDF 1.1 Turtle — causal_kg_to_turtle
text: Beckett, D. (2014). RDF 1.1 Turtle — Terse RDF Triple Language. W3C Recommendation.

title: Extract causal claims from text via an LLM backend — causal_llm_extract
text: Three backends supported via uniform prompt: "ollama"  — local, zero-cost Ollama server (default host = "http://localhost:11434", default model "gemma4:latest"). higher extraction quality switch model = "gemma4:26b". "openai" — OpenAI Chat Completions API JSON mode; needs OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable (default model "gpt-4o-mini"). "anthropic" — Claude Messages API; needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (default model "claude-sonnet-4-5"). return value backend-independent, downstream causal_llm_ingest_abstract() causal_kg_add_edge() work regardless model produced claims.

title: Ingest a corpus of abstracts into a Knowledge Graph (resumable) — causal_llm_ingest_corpus
text: Every row's (source, abstract) pair hashed stable filename; cache_dir supplied cached JSON file already exists row, LLM call skipped cached claims replayed KG. Failed rows (malformed JSON, timeouts) retried max_retries times exponential backoff. Progress reporting uses base R's txtProgressBar unless custom progress callback passed. 10 000+ abstracts cache makes workflow operationally possible: re-running function crash picks cache left , warm re-runs near- instantaneous.

what: Multi-extractor consensus over LLM-extracted causal claims — causal_llm_vote
title: Multi-extractor consensus over LLM-extracted causal claims — causal_llm_vote
text: Runs N LLM backends abstract returns single consensus data frame resolves inevitable disagreements one three voting rules. See file header design rationale.

title: Multi-extractor consensus over LLM-extracted causal claims — causal_llm_vote
code: causal_llm_vote(   abstract,   backends,   voting = c("majority", "weighted", "intersection"),   min_support = NULL,   threshold = NULL,   weights = NULL,   timeout_sec = 120 )

title: Multi-extractor consensus over LLM-extracted causal claims — causal_llm_vote
text: abstract Character scalar passage annotate. backends List backend configurations; see . voting "majority" (default), "weighted", "intersection". min_support Integer — required number backends "majority" voting. Defaults ceiling(length(backends) / 2). threshold Numeric — weighted-confidence threshold "weighted" voting. Defaults 0.5 * sum(weights). weights Optional numeric vector, one element per backend (order backends). Defaults uniform weights. timeout_sec Default per-request timeout; may overridden per-backend via timeout_sec field config entry.

title: Multi-extractor consensus over LLM-extracted causal claims — causal_llm_vote
text: data frame one row per consensus edge columns cause, effect, n_backends, mean_confidence, weighted_confidence, backends (" | "-separated list supporting backend ids), evidence (concatenated quotations).

title: Multi-extractor consensus over LLM-extracted causal claims — causal_llm_vote
text: "majority" (default) Keep edges asserted least min_support backends. min_support = NULL, defaults ceiling(length(backends) / 2) 2---3 3---5 agreement survives. "weighted" Keep edges whose weighted confidence \(\sum_i w_i c_i\) exceeds threshold. Weights supplied per backend via weights. "intersection" Keep edges asserted every backend. conservative; maximises precision expense recall.

title: Multi-extractor consensus over LLM-extracted causal claims — causal_llm_vote
text: list lists. inner list configures one backend must contain least backend field (one "ollama", "openai", "anthropic") may optionally carry model, host (Ollama), temperature, timeout_sec, api_key, id. Example: Failed backends (timeouts, missing API keys) emit warning contribute zero claims; vote continues remaining backends.
code: backends = list(   list(backend = "ollama",    model = "gemma4:latest",        host   = "http://localhost:11434"),   list(backend = "openai",    model = "gpt-4o-mini"),   list(backend = "anthropic", model = "claude-sonnet-4-6") )

title: Multi-extractor consensus over LLM-extracted causal claims — causal_llm_vote
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   backends <- list(     list(backend = "ollama",    model = "gemma4:latest"),     list(backend = "openai",    model = "gpt-4o-mini"),     list(backend = "anthropic", model = "claude-sonnet-4-6")   )   cons <- causal_llm_vote(     abstract = "In Cerrado Oxisols, precipitation drives SOC...",     backends = backends,     voting   = "majority"   )   cons } # }

what: Concurrent AGROVOC alignment for a large vocabulary — causal_ontology_agrovoc_align_batch
title: Concurrent AGROVOC alignment for a large vocabulary — causal_ontology_agrovoc_align_batch
text: Resolves vector free-text terms FAO AGROVOC SPARQL endpoint using parallel HTTP dispatch, -disk cache, retry logic exponential backoff. Designed Knowledge Graphs built thousands papers, per-term overhead causal_ontology_agrovoc_align() (~5–10 s / term agrovoc.fao.org) dominates runtime.

title: Concurrent AGROVOC alignment for a large vocabulary — causal_ontology_agrovoc_align_batch
code: causal_ontology_agrovoc_align_batch(   terms,   cache_path = NULL,   max_active = 5L,   max_per_term = 5L,   max_retries = 2L,   endpoint = "https://agrovoc.fao.org/sparql",   timeout_sec = 60L,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Concurrent AGROVOC alignment for a large vocabulary — causal_ontology_agrovoc_align_batch
text: terms Character vector free-text terms. cache_path Optional .rds file path. supplied, function reads cached matches entry writes updated matches exit. max_active Integer — maximum number concurrent HTTP connections. Default 5; raise faster throughput, lower endpoint rate-limiting . httr2 transparently pools max_active connections. max_per_term Integer — AGROVOC query LIMIT per term; closest label Levenshtein distance retained. max_retries Integer — number retry rounds terms come back non-200 response. Retries apply exponential backoff (2 ^ attempt seconds rounds). endpoint AGROVOC SPARQL endpoint URL. Override mirror local SPARQL proxy. timeout_sec Per-request timeout (seconds). verbose Logical — print one-line progress summary parallel round.

title: Concurrent AGROVOC alignment for a large vocabulary — causal_ontology_agrovoc_align_batch
text: data frame columns term, uri, label, distance (NA hit). Unresolved terms keep NA slots; caller can decide whether retry accept partial alignment.

title: Concurrent AGROVOC alignment for a large vocabulary — causal_ontology_agrovoc_align_batch
text: single composite SPARQL query binds N terms via VALUES filters labels CONTAINS(?label, ?term) theoretically optimal batching strategy, AGROVOC's production endpoint consistently rejects queries 504 gateway timeout substring predicate short-circuit bound term set. causal_ontology_agrovoc_align_batch() therefore batches transport layer instead: issues one single-term query per uncached input dispatches httr2::req_perform_parallel() max_active concurrent connections. -wire semantics identical causal_ontology_agrovoc_align(); wall-clock time changes.

title: Concurrent AGROVOC alignment for a large vocabulary — causal_ontology_agrovoc_align_batch
text: cache named list keyed normalised term, persisted single .rds file. entry every cached term short-circuited without network call. Failed terms cached, resumed run retries ; successful terms become permanent. Pointing fresh cache_path existing .rds keeps cache; passing NULL disables persistence (-memory ).

title: Concurrent AGROVOC alignment for a large vocabulary — causal_ontology_agrovoc_align_batch
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # 100-term vocabulary extracted from a KG built over 10k abstracts.   vocab <- unique(c(causal_kg_edges(kg)$cause,                      causal_kg_edges(kg)$effect))   ag <- causal_ontology_agrovoc_align_batch(     vocab,     cache_path = "tools/.agrovoc_cache.rds",     max_active = 8L,     max_retries = 2L,     verbose = TRUE   )   # Resolution rate:   mean(!is.na(ag$uri)) } # }

text: Computes Robustness Value (RV): minimum partial R-squared (exposure outcome, observed adjustments partialed ) unobserved confounder U need bring estimate zero (q = 1) reduce fraction q magnitude.  Also reports RV 5% significance (RV_q): minimum R-squared push t-ratio critical value.

text: effect Numeric; point estimate causal effect. se Numeric; standard error estimate. df Numeric; degrees freedom (n - k - 1). q Numeric; fraction estimate want neutralise. q = 1 zero-; q = 0.5 half-reduction. alpha Significance level RV_alpha (default 0.05).

title: Cinelli & Hazlett (2020) sensitivity summary for a causal effect — causal_sensitivity_summary
text: Interpretation: RV = 0.10, confounder explaining 10 percent residual variance X Y (jointly) enough kill effect.  Small RV = fragile estimate.

title: Cinelli & Hazlett (2020) sensitivity summary for a causal effect — causal_sensitivity_summary
code: # A 2SLS effect of beta = 0.008, SE = 0.003, df = 1080: causal_sensitivity_summary(0.008, 0.003, 1080) #> $effect #> [1] 0.008 #>  #> $se #> [1] 0.003 #>  #> $df #> [1] 1080 #>  #> $t_stat #> [1] 2.666667 #>  #> $rv #> [1] 0.07791866 #>  #> $rv_alpha #> [1] 0.02120882 #>  #> $q #> [1] 1 #>  #> $alpha #> [1] 0.05 #>  #> $interpretation #> [1] "An unobserved confounder explaining 7.8% of the residual variance in BOTH X and Y would suffice to zero out the estimate. At the 95% significance threshold, 2.1% is enough to make the result statistically insignificant." #>

what: Structure learning from horizon data -> Knowledge Graph — causal_structure_learn
title: Structure learning from horizon data -> Knowledge Graph — causal_structure_learn
text: Learns Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) set soil covariate / response variables directly horizon-level data frame, using one four canonical structure-learning algorithms bnlearn package (Scutari 2010). returned object edaphos_causal_kg learned DAG can () unioned LLM-extracted Knowledge Graph via causal_augment_dag(), (ii) exported RDF via causal_kg_to_turtle(), (iii) consumed backdoor-adjustment estimator via causal_kg_to_dagitty().

title: Structure learning from horizon data -> Knowledge Graph — causal_structure_learn
code: causal_structure_learn(   data,   variables = NULL,   method = c("hc", "tabu", "pc-stable", "mmhc"),   whitelist = NULL,   blacklist = NULL,   score = "bic-g",   alpha = 0.05,   bootstrap = FALSE,   R_boot = 200L,   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Structure learning from horizon data -> Knowledge Graph — causal_structure_learn
text: data data frame one row per observation (typically pedon horizon). variables Optional character vector columns include analysis. NULL, uses every numeric column data. method One "hc" (default), "tabu", "pc-stable", "mmhc". whitelist Optional data frame columns listing edges must present learned DAG. Useful pedological priors, e.g. "parent material must precede soil chemistry". blacklist Optional data frame columns listing edges must appear — typically reverse whitelist plus physically impossible arrows (e.g. soc -> elevation). score Scoring function score-based algorithms ("hc", "tabu", "mmhc"). Defaults "bic-g" (Gaussian BIC) continuous data; "bge" (Bayesian Gaussian equivalent) alternative. discrete data use "bic" "bde". alpha Significance level conditional-independence tests "pc-stable" / "mmhc". Default 0.05. bootstrap Logical — run bootstrap estimate edge confidence. Default FALSE. R_boot Integer — number bootstrap replicates. Default 200. seed Optional integer — used bootstrap resampler. verbose Logical — forwarded bnlearn.

title: Structure learning from horizon data -> Knowledge Graph — causal_structure_learn
text: edaphos_causal_kg whose source field every edge reads "structure_learn(method=...)" whose confidence either 1.0 (point learned DAG, bootstrap) bootstrap edge-frequency.

title: Structure learning from horizon data -> Knowledge Graph — causal_structure_learn
text: "hc" (default) Hill-climbing greedy search DAG space maximising Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC) score (Gaussian BIC continuous variables). Deterministic, fast, widely used. "tabu" Tabu-search variant escapes local optima keeping short memory recently visited DAGs. "pc-stable" PC-stable constraint-based algorithm (Colombo Maathuis 2014). Starts complete skeleton removes edges based partial correlation tests; returns CPDAG extend DAG via topological order consistent whitelist. "mmhc" Max-Min Hill-Climbing (Tsamardinos, Brown Aliferis 2006). Hybrid: learns skeleton constraint tests, orients edges hill-climbing BIC score.

title: Structure learning from horizon data -> Knowledge Graph — causal_structure_learn
text: bootstrap = TRUE, non-parametric bootstrap rows data performed. bootstrap replicate runs method, whitelist blacklist; fraction replicates edge appears recorded edge's confidence returned KG. gives honest uncertainty estimate learned structure, useful sample size modest relative number variables (common situation soil surveys).

title: Structure learning from horizon data -> Knowledge Graph — causal_structure_learn
text: Spirtes, P., Glymour, C. Scheines, R. (2000). Causation, Prediction, Search (2nd ed.). MIT Press. Scutari, M. (2010). Learning Bayesian networks bnlearn R package. Journal Statistical Software 35, 1–22. Colombo, D. Maathuis, M. H. (2014). Order-independent constraint-based causal structure learning. Journal Machine Learning Research 15, 3741–3782. Tsamardinos, ., Brown, L. E. Aliferis, C. F. (2006). max-min hill-climbing Bayesian network structure learning algorithm. Machine Learning 65, 31–78.

title: Structure learning from horizon data -> Knowledge Graph — causal_structure_learn
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   data(br_cerrado)   kg_learned <- causal_structure_learn(     br_cerrado,     variables = c("elev", "slope", "twi", "map_mm", "ndvi", "soc"),     method    = "hc",     whitelist = data.frame(from = c("elev", "map_mm"),                             to   = c("twi",  "soc")),     bootstrap = TRUE, R_boot = 200L, seed = 1L   )   print(kg_learned) } # }

what: edaphos: Disruptive Algorithms for Digital Soil Mapping — edaphos-package
title: edaphos: Disruptive Algorithms for Digital Soil Mapping — edaphos-package
text: edaphos research-oriented R package implements frontier algorithms Digital Soil Mapping (DSM) beyond regression-tree state art (McBratney, Mendonça Santos Minasny, 2003; Wadoux, Minasny McBratney, 2020). contributions organised across six research pillars, confronting specific methodological gap contemporary literature equipped vignette derives governing object first principles.

title: edaphos: Disruptive Algorithms for Digital Soil Mapping — edaphos-package
text: Greek \(\epsilon\delta\alpha\phi o\varsigma\) — "soil, ground."

title: edaphos: Disruptive Algorithms for Digital Soil Mapping — edaphos-package
text: 1. Causal AI. DAG-backed backdoor adjustment via dagitty — causal_clorpt_dag(), causal_cerrado_dag(), causal_adjustment_set(), causal_estimate_effect() — optional non-linear BART estimator via dbarts Suggests dependency. LLM-driven Knowledge-Graph extraction pipeline top httr2 (causal_kg_new(), causal_llm_extract(), causal_llm_ingest_corpus(), causal_augment_dag()) supports Ollama (Gemma 4), OpenAI Anthropic backends. Corpus ingestion clients SciELO (causal_corpus_scielo()) OpenAlex (causal_corpus_openalex()) transparently page upstream APIs multi-thousand-abstract pulls, deduplicated DOI / title via causal_corpus_deduplicate(). Production-grade ingestion corpora tens thousands abstracts handled causal_llm_ingest_corpus()'s cache_dir max_retries arguments (resumable, idempotent, exponential backoff). Ontology alignment curated Cerrado vocabulary live FAO AGROVOC SPARQL (causal_ontology_agrovoc_align(), causal_kg_alignment(vocab = "agrovoc")) provided -disk caching, concurrent batched alignment (causal_ontology_agrovoc_align_batch()) dispatches max_active parallel HTTP requests 10 k-node KG resolves minutes instead hours. Paper-scale KGs persisted audited via causal_kg_save() / causal_kg_load() (portable RDS edge-list), causal_kg_to_turtle() (W3C RDF 1.1 Turtle export reified provenance per edge), causal_kg_rank_edges() (multi-source ranking n_sources + mean_confidence + agrovoc_support) summary() method reports node / edge / source counts, confidence distribution DAG-ness. Multi-extractor voting (causal_llm_vote(), causal_llm_ingest_abstract_voted()) runs N LLM backends abstract resolves disagreements majority, weighted intersection rules. Bottom-structure learning (causal_structure_learn()) recovers DAG directly horizon data bnlearn bridge (hc, tabu, pc-stable, mmhc) optional bootstrap edge confidence, producing edaphos_causal_kg can unioned LLM-derived Knowledge Graph. 2. Physics-Informed ML. Parametric pedogenetic Ordinary Differential Equation integrated deSolve — piml_profile_fit(); Neural ODE differentiable Runge-Kutta integrator torch — piml_neural_ode_fit(); hierarchical covariate-conditioned Neural ODE jointly fit across pedons — piml_hierarchical_fit(). Bayesian posterior \((\lambda_0, \mu, y_\infty, y_0)\) returned piml_profile_fit_bayesian() via Laplace approximation (default) adaptive random-walk Metropolis sampler. Neural-ODE variant paired piml_neural_ode_fit_ensemble(), deep ensemble whose empirical spread approximates Bayesian predictive posterior extra torch machinery. 3. 4D Pedometry. Multi-layer stacked Convolutional LSTM sequence--one sequence--sequence training — temporal_convlstm_fit(); multi-step rollout forecasting — temporal_convlstm_rollout(); optional mass-balance physics loss; reproducible synthetic SOC dynamics cube generator — temporal_synth_soc_cube(). 4. Foundation Models. SimCLR scaffold (foundation_simclr_pretrain(), foundation_simclr_embed()) plus MoCo v2 upgrade (foundation_moco_pretrain(), foundation_moco_embed()) momentum encoder, dictionary queue raster-specific augmentation stack. planetary-scale tile pipeline (foundation_tile_source_soilgrids(), foundation_tile_source_worldclim(), foundation_tile_source_srtm(), foundation_tile_align(), foundation_tile_dataset()) streams patches lazily multi-source terra::SpatRaster mosaics; foundation_moco_pretrain_tiles() trains checkpointing -device dispatch (device = "cpu" | "mps" | "cuda"), foundation_moco_embed_raster() projects trained encoder entire AoI. downstream fine-tuning API (foundation_fit_classifier(), foundation_fit_regressor()) wraps head construction, training loop, validation split target normalisation behind single call supports linear probing full fine-tuning two-group learning-rate schedule. Public pretrained weights distributed via Zenodo: foundation_weights_list() catalogues registry, foundation_weights_download() fetches artefact SHA-256 verification caches tools::R_user_dir("edaphos"), foundation_weights_load() rebuilds edaphos_foundation_moco wrapper saved state dict. 5. Autonomous Active Learning. Closed-loop sampling policy combining Quantile-Regression-Forest uncertainty, feature-space diversity logistical cost — al_loop(), al_query(), al_initial_design(), al_fit(), al_update(); global per-location Physics- Informed rejection gates — al_physics_gate_piml(), al_physics_gate_piml_hierarchical(). Information-theoretic batch acquisition provided al_query_batchbald(), maximises mutual information batch model parameters via greedy log-det submodular objective (Kirsch, van Amersfoort Gal 2019). 6. Quantum ML. Pure-R state-vector simulator ZZFeatureMap encoding (Havlicek et al., 2019) quantum-kernel Gram matrix quantum_kernel() closed-form Kernel Ridge Regression wrapper quantum_krr_fit(). full Qiskit-backed Variational Quantum Eigensolver bridge (quantum_hamiltonian(), quantum_hamiltonian_h2(), quantum_hamiltonian_organo_mineral(), quantum_vqe_fit(), quantum_vqe_exact()) runs qiskit-aer box three interchangeable back ends: exact statevector, shot-based Aer SPSA optional noise models, full IBM Quantum Runtime dispatch (quantum_ibmq_submit(), quantum_ibmq_available(), quantum_ibmq_backends(), quantum_ibmq_least_busy()) M3 readout ZNE gate-folding mitigation (Kim et al., 2023). qiskit-nature bridge (quantum_hamiltonian_from_pyscf(), quantum_hamiltonian_organo_mineral_nature(), quantum_nature_total_energy()) lifts user-supplied XYZ geometry PySCF RHF reference, frozen-core reduction, ActiveSpace projection ParityMapper Z2 tapering, producing qubit Hamiltonian ready VQE minimisation. Curated presets cover three canonical organo-mineral motifs: carboxylate ("formic_acid"), ortho-diol ("methanediol"), monodentate Fe(III)– carboxylate coordination complex ("ferric_formate").

title: Unified posterior object for the edaphos pillars — edaphos_posterior

title: Unified posterior object for the edaphos pillars — edaphos_posterior
code: edaphos_posterior(   samples = NULL,   mean = NULL,   sd = NULL,   epistemic_sd = NULL,   aleatoric_sd = NULL,   probs = c(0.05, 0.5, 0.95),   method = c("ensemble", "bootstrap", "mcdropout", "bayesian", "loo_cv", "shots",     "analytic", "gaussian"),   query_type = c("effect", "param", "map", "feature", "sample", "energy", "other"),   units = NULL,   metadata = list(),   n_samples_if_gaussian = 500L )

title: Unified posterior object for the edaphos pillars — edaphos_posterior
text: samples Optional numeric array matrix. first axis posterior-draw axis length n_samples; remaining axes query shape. 1-D vector treated n_samples scalar draws. mean Optional numeric array query shape. samples NULL, required together sd. sd Optional numeric array query shape. epistemic_sd, aleatoric_sd Optional numeric arrays query shape; variance decomposition epistemic_sd^2 + aleatoric_sd^2 ~ sd^2 expected hold approximately (exact Gaussian posteriors). probs Numeric vector quantile probabilities pre- compute samples. Defaults c(0.05, 0.5, 0.95). method Character tag describing posterior produced, diagnostics plotting. One "ensemble", "bootstrap", "mcdropout", "bayesian", "loo_cv", "shots", "analytic", "gaussian". query_type Character tag describing posterior . One "effect", "param", "map", "feature", "sample", "energy", "". units Optional free-text tag (e.g. "g/kg", "NDVI z-units"). metadata Optional list extra provenance fields. n_samples_if_gaussian mean sd provided, constructor synthesises many Gaussian draws downstream helpers (uncertainty_calibrate, autoplot) always sample work . Defaults 500L.

title: Fine-tune or linearly probe a Pillar 4 encoder for classification — foundation_fit_classifier
code: foundation_fit_classifier(   encoder,   x,   y,   freeze_backbone = TRUE,   head = c("linear", "mlp"),   hidden = c(64L, 32L),   dropout = 0,   epochs = 30L,   batch_size = 32L,   lr = 0.001,   weight_decay = 0,   backbone_lr_mult = 0.1,   val_split = 0.2,   device = c("cpu", "mps", "cuda"),   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Fine-tune or linearly probe a Pillar 4 encoder for classification — foundation_fit_classifier
text: encoder edaphos_foundation_moco edaphos_foundation_simclr fit, bare nn_module accepts (batch, C, H, W) input returns either embedding tensor list feature field. x 4-dimensional R array (N, C, H, W) labelled patches, shape used pretrain encoder. y factor (coercible character / integer) length N patch-level class labels. freeze_backbone Logical — TRUE linear probing (default), FALSE full fine-tuning. FALSE, encoder put training mode gradients flow back classification loss. head "linear" (default) "mlp". hidden Integer vector MLP hidden-layer widths head = "mlp". Default c(64L, 32L). dropout Dropout probability MLP head (0 = disabled). epochs, batch_size, lr Training hyperparameters. weight_decay Adam weight decay (L2 regularisation). backbone_lr_mult Multiplicative factor applied lr encoder parameters freeze_backbone = FALSE. Default 0.1; set 1.0 train backbone full speed. val_split Fraction x held validation. 0 disables validation. device "cpu" (default), "mps" "cuda". requested backend unavailable function falls back "cpu" message. seed Optional integer — seeds torch, NumPy train/val split. verbose Logical — print loss / accuracy every 10 epochs.

title: Fine-tune or linearly probe a Pillar 4 encoder for classification — foundation_fit_classifier
text: edaphos_foundation_classifier list : encoder, head trained torch modules. classes character factor levels used prediction. loss_history, val_accuracy_history Per-epoch training loss validation accuracy (NA val_split = 0). config List inputs foundation_fit_classifier() reproducibility.

title: Fine-tune or linearly probe a Pillar 4 encoder for classification — foundation_fit_classifier
text: Linear probe (freeze_backbone = TRUE, head = "linear") encoder weights frozen; single nn_linear(feature_dim, n_classes) head trained. canonical benchmark evaluating self-supervised representations fixed feature extractors (, Girshick Dollar 2019). Full fine-tuning (freeze_backbone = FALSE) encoder head trained jointly, two-group learning rate (lr * backbone_lr_mult backbone, lr head). Usually better option downstream dataset large enough (≥ ~500 patches per class).

title: Fine-tune or linearly probe a Pillar 4 encoder for classification — foundation_fit_classifier
text: , K., Girshick, R. Dollar, P. (2019). Rethinking ImageNet pre-training. ICCV 2019. Kornblith, S., Shlens, J. Le, Q. V. (2019). better ImageNet models transfer better? CVPR 2019.

title: Fine-tune or linearly probe a Pillar 4 encoder for classification — foundation_fit_classifier
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   ds <- readRDS("tools/pretrain/cerrado_dataset.rds")   moco <- foundation_weights_load("edaphos-cerrado-moco-v1")    # Label a subset of patches by soil order (synthetic example).   patches <- array(rnorm(200 * ds$n_channels * 16 * 16),                    dim = c(200, ds$n_channels, 16, 16))   soil_order <- factor(sample(c("Oxisol", "Ultisol", "Inceptisol"),                                200, replace = TRUE))    fit <- foundation_fit_classifier(     moco, patches, soil_order,     freeze_backbone = TRUE, head = "linear",     epochs = 40L, device = "mps", seed = 1L   )   pred <- predict(fit, patches, type = "class") } # }

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   moco <- foundation_weights_load("edaphos-cerrado-moco-v1")   ds   <- readRDS("tools/pretrain/cerrado_dataset.rds")   patches <- array(rnorm(300 * ds$n_channels * 16 * 16),                    dim = c(300, ds$n_channels, 16, 16))   soc <- rnorm(300, mean = 15, sd = 6)   fit <- foundation_fit_regressor(     moco, patches, soc,     freeze_backbone = TRUE, head = "linear",     epochs = 40L, device = "mps", seed = 1L   )   predict(fit, patches[1:10, , , , drop = FALSE]) } # }

title: Apply a fitted MoCo v2 encoder over a full raster mosaic — foundation_moco_embed_raster

what: Pillar 4 – MoCo v2 pre-training on raster covariate patches — foundation_moco_pretrain
title: Pillar 4 – MoCo v2 pre-training on raster covariate patches — foundation_moco_pretrain
text: Self-supervised momentum-contrastive pre-training (et al. 2020; Chen et al. 2020) raster-specific augmentation stack. Compared SimCLR scaffold foundation_simclr_pretrain(), MoCo v2 introduces three architectural upgrades:

title: Pillar 4 – MoCo v2 pre-training on raster covariate patches — foundation_moco_pretrain
code: foundation_moco_pretrain(   patches,   feature_dim = 64L,   proj_dim = 32L,   queue_size = 1024L,   momentum = 0.999,   temperature = 0.07,   batch_size = 16L,   epochs = 30L,   lr = 0.03,   crop_ratio = c(0.6, 1),   flip_prob = 0.5,   rot90_prob = 0.75,   channel_drop_prob = 0.2,   cutout_prob = 0.3,   cutout_size_ratio = 0.2,   brightness_jitter = 0.2,   noise_sd = 0.1,   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Pillar 4 – MoCo v2 pre-training on raster covariate patches — foundation_moco_pretrain
text: patches 4-D array shaped (N, C, H, W) – N patches, C covariate channels, spatial H x W. feature_dim, proj_dim Integer widths backbone output contrastive projection head. queue_size Integer K – number negatives stored FIFO dictionary queue. momentum Numeric (0, 1) – EMA coefficient key encoder (MoCo paper default 0.999). temperature Numeric > 0 – InfoNCE temperature (MoCo v2 default 0.07). batch_size, epochs, lr Integer / numeric – Adam optimiser hyperparameters. crop_ratio Numeric length-2 vector – random-resized-crop ratio range. flip_prob, rot90_prob Probabilities horizontal / vertical flip 90-deg rotation. channel_drop_prob Probability zeroing given channel independently. cutout_prob, cutout_size_ratio Probability spatial cutout size ratio. brightness_jitter Numeric [0, 1) – per-channel multiplicative brightness range. noise_sd Numeric >= 0 – additive-noise standard deviation. seed, verbose elsewhere package.

title: Pillar 4 – MoCo v2 pre-training on raster covariate patches — foundation_moco_pretrain
text: edaphos_foundation_moco S3 object containing fitted query encoder (use foundation_moco_embed() extract embeddings), key encoder, training loss history configuration.

title: Pillar 4 – MoCo v2 pre-training on raster covariate patches — foundation_moco_pretrain
text: momentum key encoder updated exponential moving average: \(\theta_k \leftarrow m\,\theta_k + (1-m)\,\theta_q\). dictionary queue past keys, every mini-batch sees K negatives rather 2B - 2. wider residual CNN backbone (~feature_dim = 64 default) followed 2-layer projection head BatchNorm, matching MoCo v2 recipe. augmentation stack tuned multi-channel raster patches rather natural photographs: spatial random resized crop, horizontal / vertical flip, 90-degree rotations, per-channel Bernoulli dropout (missing-band simulation), spatial cutout (cloud-mask simulation), per-channel multiplicative brightness jitter, additive Gaussian sensor noise.

what: Dataset-backed MoCo v2 pre-training for planetary-scale corpora — foundation_moco_pretrain_tiles
title: Dataset-backed MoCo v2 pre-training for planetary-scale corpora — foundation_moco_pretrain_tiles

title: Dataset-backed MoCo v2 pre-training for planetary-scale corpora — foundation_moco_pretrain_tiles

title: Dataset-backed MoCo v2 pre-training for planetary-scale corpora — foundation_moco_pretrain_tiles

One "cpu" (default), "mps" (Apple Silicon GPU via Metal) "cuda" (NVIDIA). Requested--unavailable backends fall back "cpu" message. Added v1.2.0. checkpoint_dir Optional directory periodic checkpoints. checkpoint_every Integer – save checkpoint every k epochs. resume Optional path checkpoint directory restart .

title: Align multiple raster sources onto a common analysis grid — foundation_tile_align

Designed robust mixed resolutions (SoilGrids 250 m, WorldClim 30 arc-sec, SRTM 30 arc-sec, MODIS 250 m, ERA5 0.1 degree) MoCo v2 pretraining can consume single multi-channel tensor.

code: llm_kg_pipeline_run(   corpus_path,   output_path,   backend = c("ollama", "openai", "anthropic"),   model = NULL,   host = "http://localhost:11434",   temperature = 0,   timeout_sec = 120,   max_retries = 3L,   min_confidence = 0.5,   verbose = TRUE,   max_abstracts = NULL )

title: Run the Pilar 1 LLM-KG pipeline on a (potentially large) corpus — llm_kg_pipeline_run
text: corpus_path Character path JSONL file one {"source": ..., "abstract": ...} object per line. output_path Character path JSONL claims output. Created first run; appended resume. backend One "ollama", "openai", "anthropic". model LLM model identifier; defaults backend-appropriate. host Ollama HTTP host (ignored hosted backends). temperature Numeric; LLM sampling temperature.  Default 0. timeout_sec Numeric; per-call HTTP timeout.  Default 120. max_retries Integer; transient-error retry budget. Default 3L. min_confidence Numeric; claims confidence discarded.  Default 0.5. verbose Logical; emit progress messages.  Default TRUE. max_abstracts Optional integer; testing, cap run.

title: Run the Pilar 1 LLM-KG pipeline on a (potentially large) corpus — llm_kg_pipeline_run
text: Invisibly, list n_processed, n_skipped, n_errors, final kg object.

title: Run the Pilar 1 LLM-KG pipeline on a (potentially large) corpus — llm_kg_pipeline_run
text: every successful per-abstract extraction, claims appended output_path source identifier appended output_path.done.  llm_kg_pipeline_run() restarted output_path, abstracts whose source already .done file skipped automatically.  makes pipeline safe arbitrary process kills, network drops, Ollama restarts.

title: Run the Pilar 1 LLM-KG pipeline on a (potentially large) corpus — llm_kg_pipeline_run
text: Transient errors (HTTP 5xx, timeouts, JSON parse failures) retried max_retries times exponential back-(1s, 2s, 4s, ...).  Persistent errors logged output_path.errors skipped without halting run.

title: Pre-annotate a corpus with an LLM to produce draft claims — llm_preannotate
text: corpus Either path JSONL file one record per line (records must abstract_id abstract_text), list records already memory. backend One "ollama", "openai", "anthropic", "simulator".  "simulator" function uses llm_benchmark_simulate() pseudo-gold-standard derived abstract's first sentence – useful demos CI builds without API access.  Defaults "ollama" gemma4:latest. model Optional model id.  Defaults "gemma4:latest" Ollama. output_path Path write draft JSONL. cache_dir Optional directory per-record JSON caches. set, re-running function corpus short-circuits cached extractions, interrupted jobs resume exactly left .  Defaults NULL (cache). max_abstracts Optional integer cap many abstracts process (useful staged runs). verbose Logical; print per-abstract progress. ... Forwarded causal_llm_extract().

what: Fit a 1-D Fourier Neural Operator for depth-profile operators — no_fno_fit

title: Fit a 1-D Fourier Neural Operator for depth-profile operators — no_fno_fit
code: no_fno_fit(   depths,   targets,   covariates,   n_modes = 4L,   width = 8L,   n_blocks = 2L,   epochs = 200L,   lr = 0.01,   seed = NULL,   backend = c("r", "torch"),   device = c("cpu", "mps", "cuda") )

title: Fit a 1-D Fourier Neural Operator for depth-profile operators — no_fno_fit
text: depths Numeric vector depths (common grid, length n_depths).  Depth must equally spaced DFT exact; equally spaced, implementation falls back FFT reindexed series. targets Matrix observed profile values, shape (n_obs, n_depths).  row one site. covariates Matrix depth-dependent covariate values, shape (n_obs, n_depths, n_channels) – covariate trajectory drives operator.  2-D matrix accepted treated n_channels = 1. n_modes Integer; number Fourier modes retained spectral convolution.  Default 4L. width Integer; number latent channels.  Default 8L. n_blocks Integer; number FNO blocks.  Default 2L. epochs Integer; SGD epochs. lr Learning rate. seed RNG seed. backend "r" (default, pure-R ELM-style) "torch" (full autograd via torch::optim_adam; requires torch Suggests dependency).  v2.7.0 upgrade. device "cpu" (default), "mps" (Apple Silicon) "cuda" backend = "torch".

Assumed constant within pedon — first row pedon used. hidden Integer vector, MLP widths ODE vector field \(f_\theta\). y0_hidden Integer vector, MLP widths surface-value head \(y_0(\mathbf{x})\). n_steps Integer, RK4 steps successive observation depths. epochs, lr Integer numeric — Adam hyperparameters. seed, verbose elsewhere edaphos.

what: Fit a Neural ODE depth profile (Pillar 2, deep variant) — piml_neural_ode_fit
title: Fit a Neural ODE depth profile (Pillar 2, deep variant) — piml_neural_ode_fit
text: Models vector field \(dy/dz = f_\theta(z, y)\) \(f_\theta\) small MLP (default 2 x 16 + tanh). forward model fixed-step RK4 integrator runs end--end torch, training drives MLP weights — , optionally, surface value \(y_0\) — back-propagating whole integration (proper Neural ODE, Chen et al. 2018).

title: Fit a Neural ODE depth profile (Pillar 2, deep variant) — piml_neural_ode_fit

title: Fit a Neural ODE depth profile (Pillar 2, deep variant) — piml_neural_ode_fit
text: depths Numeric vector horizon mid-depths. values Numeric vector observed values, length. y_surface Optional numeric; supplied, $y_0$ fixed (learnable parameter) — used trust specific 0 cm reading. hidden Integer vector MLP hidden-layer widths. n_steps Integer, RK4 steps successive observation depths. Higher = accurate, slower. epochs, lr Integer numeric — training hyperparameters Adam. seed Optional integer — forwarded torch::torch_manual_seed reproducibility. verbose Logical; print training loss every 50 epochs.

title: Fit a Neural ODE depth profile (Pillar 2, deep variant) — piml_neural_ode_fit

what: Train a deep ensemble of Neural ODEs for uncertainty quantification — piml_neural_ode_fit_ensemble
title: Train a deep ensemble of Neural ODEs for uncertainty quantification — piml_neural_ode_fit_ensemble
text: Wraps piml_neural_ode_fit() K-member ensemble, member trained independent random initialisation. returned object behaves like single edaphos_piml_neural_ode purposes predict() — except method returns full K × length(newdepths) matrix member-wise predictions, (optionally) tidy (mean, sd, lower, upper) credible-interval summary.

title: Train a deep ensemble of Neural ODEs for uncertainty quantification — piml_neural_ode_fit_ensemble
code: piml_neural_ode_fit_ensemble(   depths,   values,   y_surface = NULL,   K = 5L,   hidden = c(16L, 16L),   n_steps = 4L,   epochs = 500L,   lr = 0.01,   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Train a deep ensemble of Neural ODEs for uncertainty quantification — piml_neural_ode_fit_ensemble
text: depths, values, y_surface, hidden, n_steps, epochs, lr, verbose Forwarded piml_neural_ode_fit() member--member. See details. K Integer — number ensemble members. Default 5 reasonable speed/variance trade-; 10 recommended ceiling laptop-scale problems. seed Optional integer — seeds ensemble. Member k trained seed = seed + k - 1L.

title: Train a deep ensemble of Neural ODEs for uncertainty quantification — piml_neural_ode_fit_ensemble
text: edaphos_piml_neural_ode_ensemble carrying: members list K fitted edaphos_piml_neural_ode objects. K, hidden, n_steps, y_surface Configuration echo. fitted K × n_obs matrix -sample predictions. fitted_mean, fitted_sd Ensemble mean / standard deviation fitted. rmse RMSE ensemble mean training observations.

title: Train a deep ensemble of Neural ODEs for uncertainty quantification — piml_neural_ode_fit_ensemble
text: theoretical justification ensemble posterior approximation developed Lakshminarayanan et al. 2017 Wilson & Izmailov 2020 (see @references section). short: wide neural networks, different SGD trajectories converge different basins loss surface, resulting member-wise spread well-calibrated proxy Bayesian posterior predictive variance.

title: Train a deep ensemble of Neural ODEs for uncertainty quantification — piml_neural_ode_fit_ensemble
text: Lakshminarayanan, B., Pritzel, . Blundell, C. (2017). Simple scalable predictive uncertainty estimation using deep ensembles. NeurIPS 30, 6402–6413. Wilson, . G. Izmailov, P. (2020). Bayesian deep learning probabilistic perspective generalization. NeurIPS 33, 4697–4708.

title: Train a deep ensemble of Neural ODEs for uncertainty quantification — piml_neural_ode_fit_ensemble
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   depths <- c(5, 15, 30, 60, 100)   values <- c(25, 18, 12, 8, 6.5)   ens <- piml_neural_ode_fit_ensemble(depths, values, K = 5L,                                         epochs = 300L, seed = 1L)   predict(ens, newdepths = c(10, 20, 40, 80), interval = 0.95) } # }

what: Fit a Physics-Informed depth-profile model (Pillar 2) — piml_profile_fit
title: Fit a Physics-Informed depth-profile model (Pillar 2) — piml_profile_fit
text: Fits ODE $$\frac{dy}{dz} = -\lambda_0 e^{-\mu z} (y - y_\infty)$$ one soil pedon minimising sum squared errors ODE-predicted observed values, L2 regulariser (re-parametrised) parameters. Physics encoded forward model — fit can never produce profile violates prescribed exponential-asymptote dynamics.

title: Fit a Physics-Informed depth-profile model (Pillar 2) — piml_profile_fit

title: Fit a Physics-Informed depth-profile model (Pillar 2) — piml_profile_fit
text: depths Numeric vector horizon mid-depths (units, e.g. centimetres surface). values Numeric vector observed property values, length depths. y_surface Optional numeric; supplied, surface value y(0) = y_surface fixed optimised. Use reliable 0 cm observation (laboratory -horizon value). start Optional named numeric vector starting parameters (log_lambda0, mu, y_inf, optionally y0). NULL, data-driven guess built observed range depths. reg Numeric L2 regularisation strength parameter vector. control List passed stats::optim's control argument (default list(maxit = 2000)).

title: Fit a Physics-Informed depth-profile model (Pillar 2) — piml_profile_fit
text: edaphos_piml_profile object components: params Named list fitted lambda0, mu, y_inf, y0. theta unconstrained parameter vector optimiser found. objective Final regularised SSE loss. converged Logical, whether optim reported convergence. depths, values, y_surface Inputs echoed back. rmse Unregularised RMSE training data.

title: Fit a Physics-Informed depth-profile model (Pillar 2) — piml_profile_fit
code: depths <- c(5, 15, 30, 60, 100) values <- c(25, 18, 12, 8, 6.5)   # e.g. SOC (g/kg) decreasing with depth fit <- piml_profile_fit(depths, values) fit #> <edaphos_piml_profile> #>   dy/dz = -lambda0 * exp(-mu*z) * (y - y_inf) #>   lambda0 = 0.04846   mu = 0.003277 #>   y_inf   = 6.162     y0 = 30.08    #>   n obs   = 5         rmse = 0.09798 #>   converged = TRUE  piml_profile_predict(fit$params, c(10, 50)) #> [1] 21.009577  8.720935

title: Bayesian posterior for the Pillar 2 pedogenetic ODE — piml_profile_fit_bayesian
text: depths, values Numeric vectors — piml_profile_fit(). y_surface Optional fixed surface value. method One "laplace" (default) "mcmc". prior Named list hyperparameters priors. See source default structure (fields log_lambda0_mean, log_lambda0_sd, mu_sd, y_inf_mean, y_inf_sd, y0_mean, y0_sd). start Optional starting vector. Defaults piml_profile_fit()'s point-estimate theta MAP search. control optim control list MAP step. n_iter, n_burn, thin, seed MCMC settings. consulted method = "mcmc". verbose Logical — print one progress line per 10% MCMC iterations.

title: Bayesian posterior for the Pillar 2 pedogenetic ODE — piml_profile_fit_bayesian
text: edaphos_piml_bayes object : method "laplace" "mcmc". map Named list MAP values natural parameters (lambda0, mu, y_inf, y0). theta_map Unconstrained parameter vector MAP. sigma Observation-noise standard deviation estimated MAP. cov \(d \times d\) posterior covariance matrix unconstrained scale (Laplace), empirical sample covariance MCMC chain. draws M--d matrix posterior samples unconstrained scale. Laplace, 2000 draws pre-sampled \(N(\text{map}, \text{cov})\) predictive convenience; MCMC, kept post-burn-chain. summary data frame mean, sd, q2.5, q50, q97.5 per parameter (natural scale: lambda0, mu, y_inf, y0). accept_rate MCMC acceptance rate (method = "mcmc").

title: Bayesian posterior for the Pillar 2 pedogenetic ODE — piml_profile_fit_bayesian
text: method = "laplace" (default) Gaussian posterior obtained MAP inverse observed information MAP. Accurate posterior approximately Gaussian, typical well-identified profiles \(n \geq 4\) horizons. Runtime: O(milliseconds). method = "mcmc" Adaptive random-walk Metropolis (Haario, Saksman Tamminen 2001; see @references section ). Proposal covariance starts Laplace covariance, scaled Roberts-Gelman-Gilks \((2.38)^2 / d\) factor, updated online Haario recursion warm-period. Returns full posterior samples non-Gaussian / multimodal posteriors captured faithfully. Runtime: seconds default 5000 iterations. noise scale \(\sigma\) estimated MAP residual RMSE held fixed MCMC (empirical Bayes). Weakly-informative, data-driven priors applied default; pass custom prior list override .

title: Bayesian posterior for the Pillar 2 pedogenetic ODE — piml_profile_fit_bayesian
text: Bishop, C. M. (2006). Pattern Recognition Machine Learning. Springer, chapter 4.4 (Laplace approximation). Haario, H., Saksman, E. Tamminen, J. (2001). adaptive Metropolis algorithm. Bernoulli 7, 223–242.

title: Physics-informed quantum kernel via ODE-residual fusion — piml_quantum_kernel
text: X Numeric matrix (rows = samples, columns = features encode ZZFeatureMap).  Features already [0, pi] – use quantum_scale() needed. y Numeric response vector (one per row X) used compute residuals ODE fit. depths Numeric vector depths (length y) observations taken. ode_fit fitted object piml_profile_fit() piml_profile_fit_bayesian() providing predict() method. alpha Numeric [0, 1]; mixing weight.  Default 0.7 (quantum-heavy, physics-informed physics-dominated). sigma Numeric; RBF bandwidth residual scale. Default: median absolute residual training set (Silverman's rule thumb). reps Integer; ZZFeatureMap repetitions (forwarded quantum_kernel()). backend Forwarded quantum_kernel(); "rcpp" default.

title: Physics-informed quantum kernel via ODE-residual fusion — piml_quantum_kernel
text: PSD matrix shape (n, n).

title: Physics-informed quantum kernel via ODE-residual fusion — piml_quantum_kernel
text: $$K_{PI}(x_i, x_j) = \alpha\, K_{quantum}(x_i, x_j) + (1 - \alpha)\,       \exp\!\Bigl(-\frac{(e_i - e_j)^2}{2\,\sigma^2}\Bigr)$$ \(e_i = y_i - \hat y_{ODE}(z_i, x_i)\) residual observed \(y_i\) ODE-predicted value depth \(z_i\).  output PSD \(\alpha \[0, 1]\).

title: Physics-informed quantum kernel via ODE-residual fusion — piml_quantum_kernel
text: Bishop, T. F. . et al. (1999). Modelling soil attribute depth functions equal-area quadratic smoothing splines. Geoderma 91, 27-45.

what: Build a quantum Hamiltonian from a molecular geometry — quantum_hamiltonian_from_pyscf
title: Build a quantum Hamiltonian from a molecular geometry — quantum_hamiltonian_from_pyscf
text: End--end ab initio pipeline: restricted Hartree-Fock reference computed PySCFDriver requested atomic-orbital basis (STO-3G default — minimal, illustrative, small enough laptop-scale VQE). Optionally, chemical core orbitals frozen FreezeCoreTransformer — cheap orbital-space-neutral reduction typically halves qubit count first-row organics. frontier orbitals projected (num_active_electrons, num_active_orbitals) active space via ActiveSpaceTransformer, keeping chemistry local coordination event user cares (e.g. Fe-O-C charge transfer organo-mineral complex). resulting second-quantised Hamiltonian mapped qubits chosen fermion--qubit transformation; default ParityMapper used known particle numbers, taper two qubits Z2 symmetry reduction.

title: Build a quantum Hamiltonian from a molecular geometry — quantum_hamiltonian_from_pyscf
code: quantum_hamiltonian_from_pyscf(   atom,   basis = "sto3g",   charge = 0L,   spin = 0L,   freeze_core = TRUE,   num_active_electrons = NULL,   num_active_orbitals = NULL,   mapper = c("parity", "jordan_wigner", "bravyi_kitaev") )

title: Build a quantum Hamiltonian from a molecular geometry — quantum_hamiltonian_from_pyscf
text: atom Character — PySCF atom specification, one atom per semicolon-separated block, e.g. "H 0 0 0; H 0 0 0.735" (H2 0.735 Angstroms) "C 0 0 0; O 1.21 0 0; O -0.63 1.08 0; H -0.3 -0.99 0; H -1.56 0.85 0" (formic acid). basis Character — atomic-orbital basis set. Default "sto3g". Use "631g", "ccpvdz", larger chemistry- grade accuracy cost qubits. charge Integer — molecular charge. Default 0. spin Integer — 2S, .e. number unpaired electrons. Default 0 (closed shell). freeze_core Logical — apply frozen-core approximation active-space transformation. Default TRUE. num_active_electrons Optional integer — size active electron space (freezing core). NULL, active-space reduction applied (frontier orbitals kept). num_active_orbitals Optional integer — size active orbital space. mapper Character — fermion--qubit mapping. One "parity" (default, tapering particle-number symmetry), "jordan_wigner", "bravyi_kitaev".

title: Build a quantum Hamiltonian from a molecular geometry — quantum_hamiltonian_from_pyscf
text: edaphos_quantum_hamiltonian carrying Pauli-string Hamiltonian active-space problem. object additionally exposes following attributes: nuclear_repulsion_energy Nuclear-nuclear Coulomb repulsion (constant). frozen_core_shift Constant energy shift introduced frozen-core transformation (0 freeze_core = FALSE). reference_energy Hartree-Fock reference energy active-space problem. num_particles Length-2 integer vector c(alpha, beta) — number spin-spin-electrons active space. num_spatial_orbitals Number active spatial orbitals. geometry, basis, charge, spin, mapper Echoed inputs.

title: Build a quantum Hamiltonian from a molecular geometry — quantum_hamiltonian_from_pyscf
text: returned object standard edaphos_quantum_hamiltonian can dropped straight quantum_vqe_fit() quantum_ibmq_submit(). additionally exposes full set energy shifts attributes user can recompute total molecular energy VQE result: $$E_\mathrm{total} = \langle H \rangle_\mathrm{VQE} +                         E_\mathrm{shift}$$ \(E_\mathrm{shift} = E_\mathrm{nuc} +                         E_\mathrm{frozen} +                         E_\mathrm{active}\) sums nuclear-repulsion constant, frozen-core shift (zero freeze_core = FALSE) active-space projection shift (zero active-space transformation applied). helper quantum_nature_total_energy() performs reconstruction fit object.

title: Organo-mineral Hamiltonians derived from ab initio molecular models — quantum_hamiltonian_organo_mineral_nature
text: "formic_acid" (default) HCOOH STO-3G chemical core frozen (2e, 2o) active space. Models carboxylate functional group dominant coordinating moiety humic acids. parity tapering qubit Hamiltonian 2 qubits / ~5 Pauli terms — fast enough run hardware small enough CI. "methanediol" H\(_2\)C(OH)\(_2\) STO-3G core frozen (2e, 2o) active space. Models ortho-diol motif underpins catechol-type Fe(III) chelation humic substances. 2 qubits tapering. "ferric_formate" Monodentate Fe(III)–OOCH complex STO-3G. Open-shell (S = 5/2), requires (4e, 4o) active space around Fe 3d + carboxylate \(\pi^*\), produces 4-qubit Hamiltonian parity tapering. variant minimum viable representation organo-mineral coordination event clay–humus interface; SCF convergence sensitive initial guess recommend running backend = "aer_shots" "ibmq" "SPSA" least 100 iterations, consulting attr(ham, "reference_energy") sanity check.

Y Optional numeric matrix / data frame number columns X. NULL (default), symmetric Gram matrix K(X, X) returned. reps Integer encoding depth — forwarded quantum_feature_map(). backend One "rcpp" (default, 10-50x faster; requires package properly installed compiled code available) "r" (pure-R reference implementation, kept audit fallback).

what: Variational Quantum Eigensolver (Pillar 6 main entry point) — quantum_vqe_fit
title: Variational Quantum Eigensolver (Pillar 6 main entry point) — quantum_vqe_fit
text: Runs Peruzzo et al. 2014 VQE supplied Hamiltonian hardware-efficient EfficientSU2 ansatz (Kandala et al. 2017) classical optimiser (default COBYLA). optimisation trajectory captured callback energy curve can plotted audited fact.

title: Variational Quantum Eigensolver (Pillar 6 main entry point) — quantum_vqe_fit
code: quantum_vqe_fit(   hamiltonian,   ansatz_reps = 2L,   optimizer = c("COBYLA", "SPSA", "SLSQP", "L-BFGS-B"),   max_iter = 200L,   backend = c("aer_statevector", "aer_shots", "ibmq"),   shots = NULL,   mitigation = c("none", "m3", "zne"),   ibmq_backend = NULL,   seed = NULL,   initial_point = NULL )

title: Variational Quantum Eigensolver (Pillar 6 main entry point) — quantum_vqe_fit
text: hamiltonian edaphos_quantum_hamiltonian. ansatz_reps Integer — number EfficientSU2 repetition blocks. blocks increase expressivity cost variational parameters. optimizer Character — one "COBYLA" (default), "SPSA", "SLSQP", "L-BFGS-B". Shot-based hardware runs strongly prefer "SPSA" robust stochastic cost-function evaluations (Spall 1998). max_iter Integer — maximum classical-optimiser iterations. backend Character — one "aer_statevector" (default, exact), "aer_shots" (shot-based simulation), "ibmq" (real IBM Quantum hardware). See Details. shots Integer — number circuit shots per energy evaluation. Ignored backend = "aer_statevector". Defaults 4096 "aer_shots" "ibmq" left NULL. mitigation Character — one "none" (default), "m3", "zne". Controls hardware error mitigation; see Details. ibmq_backend Character — name target IBM Quantum backend (e.g. "ibm_brisbane", "ibm_sherbrooke") backend = "ibmq". NULL, least-busy operational backend available account selected automatically. seed Optional integer — seeds NumPy / Qiskit RNGs reproducible runs. initial_point Optional numeric vector — custom starting ansatz parameters. Defaults Qiskit's random initial point.

title: Variational Quantum Eigensolver (Pillar 6 main entry point) — quantum_vqe_fit
text: edaphos_quantum_vqe object : energy Ground-state energy estimate. exact Numerically exact ground-state energy, reference. gap Absolute difference |energy - exact|. parameters Numeric vector optimal ansatz parameters. history Numeric vector energy values, one per optimiser iteration (via callback). n_iter Integer iteration count. shots, mitigation Echo execution configuration. hamiltonian,ansatz,optimizer,backend Configuration echo.

title: Variational Quantum Eigensolver (Pillar 6 main entry point) — quantum_vqe_fit
text: Three execution back ends supported v0.9.0: "aer_statevector" (default) Exact noiseless statevector simulation via qiskit.primitives.StatevectorEstimator. ansatz transpiled. Deterministic; limited memory roughly 24 qubits. "aer_shots" Shot-based simulation via qiskit_aer.primitives.EstimatorV2. energy evaluation estimated shots circuit executions therefore carries finite-sample noise order 1/sqrt(shots). ansatz transpiled standard \{id, rz, sx, x, cx, u\} basis gate set Aer primitive can dispatch . "ibmq" Real hardware via qiskit_ibm_runtime.EstimatorV2 inside Runtime Session. Requires qiskit-ibm-runtime Python package IBMQ_TOKEN environment variable. Supports two mitigation strategies (Kim et al. 2023, see References): mitigation = "m3" — Matrix-free Measurement Mitigation (TREX + M3 readout correction), mapped IBM runtime resilience_level = 1; mitigation = "zne" — Zero-Noise Extrapolation gate-folding noise scales, mapped IBM runtime resilience_level = 2.

title: Variational Quantum Eigensolver (Pillar 6 main entry point) — quantum_vqe_fit
text: Peruzzo, . et al. (2014). variational eigenvalue solver photonic quantum processor. Nature Communications 5, 4213. Kandala, . et al. (2017). Hardware-efficient variational quantum eigensolver small molecules quantum magnets. Nature 549, 242–246. Kim, Y. et al. (2023). Evidence utility quantum computing fault tolerance. Nature 618, 500–505. Spall, J. C. (1998). Implementation simultaneous perturbation algorithm stochastic optimisation. IEEE Transactions Aerospace Electronic Systems 34, 817–823.

title: Variational Quantum Eigensolver (Pillar 6 main entry point) — quantum_vqe_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   ham <- quantum_hamiltonian_h2()    # 1) Exact noiseless reference (fast).   fit <- quantum_vqe_fit(ham, backend = "aer_statevector", seed = 1L)    # 2) Shot-based simulation with SPSA (honest to finite sampling).   fit_s <- quantum_vqe_fit(ham, backend = "aer_shots",                             shots = 4096L, optimizer = "SPSA",                             max_iter = 100L, seed = 1L)    # 3) Real IBM Quantum hardware with M3 readout mitigation.   if (quantum_ibmq_available()) {     fit_q <- quantum_vqe_fit(ham, backend = "ibmq",                               shots = 4096L,                               mitigation = "m3",                               ibmq_backend = "ibm_brisbane",                               optimizer = "SPSA",                               max_iter = 50L)   } } # }

what: Fit a stacked ConvLSTM on a 4D covariate cube — temporal_convlstm_fit
title: Fit a stacked ConvLSTM on a 4D covariate cube — temporal_convlstm_fit
text: Trains multi-layer ConvLSTM + 1x1 head input tensor shaped (batch, T, C, H, W). Supports two objectives: sequence--one (return_sequence = FALSE, default) — target (batch, H, W), typically last-month property; sequence--sequence (return_sequence = TRUE) — target (batch, T, H, W), setup used temporal_convlstm_rollout() forecast whole horizon future driver channels known (e.g. weather forecasts).

title: Fit a stacked ConvLSTM on a 4D covariate cube — temporal_convlstm_fit
code: temporal_convlstm_fit(   sequence,   target,   hidden_dims = 4L,   kernel_size = 3L,   return_sequence = FALSE,   epochs = 80L,   lr = 0.01,   physics_lambda = 0,   physics_k_in = 0.03,   physics_k_out = 0.015,   physics_driver_channel = 2L,   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Fit a stacked ConvLSTM on a 4D covariate cube — temporal_convlstm_fit
text: sequence R array torch_tensor shape (batch, T, C, H, W). target R array tensor; shape depends return_sequence. hidden_dims Integer vector — one entry per ConvLSTM layer. Length determines depth. Default c(4L) (single-layer). kernel_size Integer, spatial kernel size (odd). return_sequence Logical; see objectives . epochs, lr Adam hyperparameters. physics_lambda Numeric >= 0; weight physics-informed mass-balance regulariser. > 0 (return_sequence = TRUE), loss becomes $$\mathrm{MSE}(\hat y, y) + \lambda_{\text{phys}}\,          \mathrm{MSE}\!\left(\Delta\hat y_t,\,          k_{\text{}} P_t - k_{\text{}} \hat y_t P_t / \bar P\right),$$ .e. predicted per-step change \(\Delta\hat y_t =   \hat y_{t+1} - \hat y_t\) pushed towards mass-balance increment implied driver channel. Pillar 2 × Pillar 3 fusion — Physics-Informed ConvLSTM. physics_k_in, physics_k_out Numeric rate coefficients mass-balance prior normalised units model sees training time. Setting zero collapses physics loss pure temporal smoothness penalty. physics_driver_channel Integer, index (1-based) input channel carries driver \(P_t\) (e.g. precipitation). seed Optional integer reproducibility. verbose Logical; print loss every 10 epochs.
