what: Ensemble Kalman update of a Pillar 3 forecast by new point observations — temporal_kalman_update
title: Ensemble Kalman update of a Pillar 3 forecast by new point observations — temporal_kalman_update
text: Nudges temporal_convlstm_rollout forecast toward set new -situ observations known grid coordinates, using stochastic Ensemble Kalman Filter Evensen (1994) Burgers, van Leeuwen Evensen (1998).

title: Ensemble Kalman update of a Pillar 3 forecast by new point observations — temporal_kalman_update
code: temporal_kalman_update(   forecast_ensemble,   obs_value,   obs_row,   obs_col,   obs_sd,   time_step = 1L,   localization_radius = NULL,   seed = NULL )

title: Ensemble Kalman update of a Pillar 3 forecast by new point observations — temporal_kalman_update
text: forecast_ensemble 3-D array (N_ens, H, W) (single-step forecast) 4-D array (N_ens, H, W, T) (multi-step). 4-D input update applied time_step. obs_value Numeric vector length n_obs — observed values assimilate. obs_row, obs_col Integer vectors length n_obs — row / column indices (1-based, row-major top-left grid) observations taken. obs_sd Numeric scalar vector length n_obs — standard deviation observation. Sets diagonal observation-noise covariance matrix \(R\). time_step Integer — forecast_ensemble 4-D, time slice update. Ignored 3-D input. localization_radius Optional numeric — supplied, applies Gaspari-Cohn (1999) 5th-order polynomial taper Kalman gain function grid-cell distance observation. taper 1 distance 0, half-bandwidth localization_radius cells, identically zero beyond 2 * localization_radius cells. Typical choices localization_radius = 2..5 cells ensembles K = 5..30 range. default NULL disables localization (pure stochastic EnKF). seed Optional integer — seeds Gaussian perturbations.

title: Ensemble Kalman update of a Pillar 3 forecast by new point observations — temporal_kalman_update
text: list : analysis_ensemble updated ensemble, shape forecast_ensemble. analysis_mean, analysis_sd Pointwise posterior mean / standard deviation across ensemble members, updated time step. gain_row_norm diagnostics: \(L_2\) norm column Kalman gain, one per observation. Large values indicate observations moved posterior lot. innovation vector \(y - H \bar X_f\) observation-minus-forecast differences, standard EnKF diagnostic.

title: Ensemble Kalman update of a Pillar 3 forecast by new point observations — temporal_kalman_update
text: forecast must ensemble: stochastic EnKF treats first dimension forecast_ensemble ensemble axis (size \(N_{\mathrm{ens}}\)). point estimate (single-member "ensemble") supported update collapses deterministic nudge posterior uncertainty recovered single run. Algorithm (stochastic EnKF). ensemble member \(X_f^{()}\): Draw observation perturbation \(\varepsilon^{()} \sim \mathcal N(0, R)\). Compute Kalman gain \(K = P_f H^T (H P_f H^T + R)^{-1}\), \(P_f\) estimated ensemble \(H\) linear operator mapping full grid observed cells. Analysis step: \(X_a^{()} = X_f^{()} + K (y + \varepsilon^{()} - H X_f^{()})\).

title: Ensemble Kalman update of a Pillar 3 forecast by new point observations — temporal_kalman_update
text: small ensembles (N_ens < ~100), raw sample covariance develops spurious long-range correlations drag analysis away truth far observations. optional localization_radius argument applies Gaspari-Cohn (1999) 5th-order polynomial taper Kalman gain, zeroing update outside neighbourhood observation. Without localization (localization_radius = NULL, default), small ensembles often exhibit classic ensemble-collapse pathology analysis RMSE grows prior RMSE even posterior spread shrinks.

title: Ensemble Kalman update of a Pillar 3 forecast by new point observations — temporal_kalman_update
text: Evensen, G. (1994). Sequential data assimilation nonlinear quasi-geostrophic model using Monte Carlo methods forecast error statistics. Journal Geophysical Research: Oceans 99(C5), 10143-10162. Burgers, G., van Leeuwen, P. J. Evensen, G. (1998). Analysis scheme ensemble Kalman filter. Monthly Weather Review 126, 1719-1724.

title: Ensemble Kalman update of a Pillar 3 forecast by new point observations — temporal_kalman_update
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Forecast ensemble from a ConvLSTM with K members   fc <- array(rnorm(10 * 20 * 20), dim = c(10, 20, 20))    # Three in-situ observations at known grid cells   assim <- temporal_kalman_update(     forecast_ensemble = fc,     obs_value = c(0.62, 0.58, 0.51),     obs_row   = c(5L, 10L, 15L),     obs_col   = c(5L, 10L, 15L),     obs_sd    = 0.02,     seed      = 1L   )   assim$analysis_mean          # posterior mean field   assim$analysis_sd            # posterior SD field } # }

text: post edaphos_posterior. truth Numeric array shape post$mean; ground-truth values. probs Nominal coverage probabilities report PICP + MPIW, reliability curve. Defaults seq(0.05, 0.95, = 0.05).

title: Calibration diagnostics for an edaphos_posterior — uncertainty_calibrate
text: list : crps Mean sample-based CRPS across query cells. picp Named vector empirical coverage, one per probs. mpiw Named vector mean interval widths, one per probs. reliability_df Data frame columns nominal, empirical, diff suitable ggplot2::geom_line. point_rmse Root-mean-squared error posterior mean truth.

title: Calibration diagnostics for an edaphos_posterior — uncertainty_calibrate
text: CRPS sample-based posterior F scalar truth y computed Monte-Carlo formula \(   \mathrm{CRPS}(F, y) = \tfrac{1}{N}\sum_i |s_i - y|                          - \tfrac{1}{2N^2}\sum_{,j} |s_i - s_j| \) (see Gneiting & Raftery 2007 derivation strictly proper scoring rule interpretation). per-query CRPS averaged across query cells single reported scalar.

title: Calibration diagnostics for an edaphos_posterior — uncertainty_calibrate
text: Gneiting, T. Raftery, . E. (2007). Strictly proper scoring rules, prediction, estimation. Journal American Statistical Association 102(477), 359-378.

what: Pilar 1 – production harness for 10k+ LLM-KG extraction runs
title: edaphos 3.10.0
text: causal_llm_extract() (per-abstract LLM primitive shipped v1.4) now joined multi-hour, multi-thousand-abstract ORCHESTRATOR resumable JSONL checkpointing, throttle/retry, pre-flight backend validation: Reads JSONL corpus ({"source": ..., "abstract": ...} per line). Calls causal_llm_extract() per abstract supplied backend (Ollama / OpenAI / Anthropic). Streams per-abstract claims output_path. Maintains output_path.done sorted set completed source IDs – re-run, already-done sources skipped (RESUMABLE). transient errors (HTTP 5xx, timeouts, JSON parse failures) retries max_retries times exponential back-(1s, 2s, 4s). Persistent errors logged output_path.errors without halting run. Builds -memory edaphos_causal_kg ready causal_kg_to_dagitty() / causal_kg_to_turtle(). llm_kg_ollama_check(host, model) – pre-flight gate HEAD-pings Ollama server, lists installed models, reports whether named model present.

title: edaphos 3.6.0
text: Replaces v2.6.0 (seq_len(n)) per-node softmax loop inside .gnn_gat_layer() fully vectorised sparse-matrix formulation: Compute attention scores every edge single s_l[src] + s_r[dst] leaky-ReLU + edge-weight scaling. Build sparse matrix Matrix::sparseMatrix(= src,      j = dst, x = alpha, dims = c(n, n)) aggregate .matrix(%*% Wh). Isolated-node fallback (outgoing edges) preserved verbatim v2.6.0 path. what: Pilar 7 Gibbs sampler: triangular-solve MVN sampling
title: edaphos 3.2.0
text: v2.3.0 Gibbs loop built n x n posterior-covariance matrix V_w = (R_inv / sigma^2 + / tau^2)^-1 computing chol2inv() every iteration, drew w via SECOND Cholesky V_w. Profiling n = 300 showed 36 % wall-time chol.default 30 % chol2inv. v3.2.0 fast path never forms V_w. instead Computes single upper-triangular Cholesky L = chol(P_w) precision matrix (existing .chol_jitter() retry guard), Draws sample via two triangular solves + rnorm(n): mu_w = backsolve(L, backsolve(L, rhs, transpose = TRUE)), w = mu_w + backsolve(L, z), z ~ N(0, ). Covariance provably V_w without ever inverting: Var(backsolve(L, z)) = solve(L) solve(L)' = (L'L)^-1 = P_w^-1 = V_w. transform applied beta update (precision = X'X / tau^2 + prior_prec * ), two diag(...) calls rebuilding identity + scalar matrices iteration replaced -place diag<- updates. Measured speedup: 2.5x n = 300, nmcmc = 500 (v2.3.0: 7.06 s / v3.2.0: 2.81 s). Posterior recoverability v2.3.0 test-suite guarantees preserved.

previous_headings: Performance: Gibbs fast path + batched DDPM training
what: Pilar 9 DDPM training: batched forward + gradient accumulation
title: edaphos 3.2.0
text: v2.5.0 training loop iterated (seq_len(n_patches)), calling .dm_forward() per patch accumulating gradient row--row outer(). v3.2.0 introduces .dm_forward_batch() processes full minibatch three GEMMs: H1 = ReLU(Z W1 + b1) – single matmul + row-broadcast H2 = ReLU(H1 W2 + b2) Eps_hat = H2 W3 + b3 replaces per-row outer-product accumulator single crossprod(H2, Resid). Gradients bit-identical (max |diff| < 2e-15 n = 128, verified tests) epoch wall-time ~4-6x faster across n_patches [64, 512].

title: edaphos 3.0.0
text: Six new scientifically motivated bridge functions compose two ten research pillars single API. six follow v2.1.1 *_query_* signature pattern (established *_fit / *_loss convention) drop existing loops boilerplate. Active-learning bridges (feed Pilar 5’s query step posterior-like uncertainty neighbouring pilares): al_query_neural_operator() (P8 x P5) – ranks candidate sites disagreement DeepONet / FNO operator prediction Pilar 2 pedogenetic ODE, normalised per-site perturbation-spread uncertainty. Returns edaphos_al_neural_operator_query data frame. al_query_diffusion() (P9 x P5) – Monte Carlo posterior- sampling conditional DDPM, ranks candidate cells per- cell standard deviation across draws. Optional combine argument weights SD absolute posterior mean. Returns edaphos_al_diffusion_query. al_query_bhs() (P7 x P5) – Settles (2009, Sec. 3.3) style Thompson-sampling AL driven BHS posterior MCMC draws. Computes s2 * (1 - k' R_inv k) + t2 averaged across draws selected sites high predictive variance MANY plausible posterior parameterisations. Structural bridges (compose one pilar’s representation another’s learning step): gnn_causal_discovery() (P10 x P1) – augments feature data frame GAT node embeddings nuisance conditioners runs causal_structure_learn() extended frame. returned DAG restricted edges user-named variables via kg$edges_feature_only; embeddings job absorbers spatial dependence expert enumerate. temporal_piml_loss() (P2 x P3) – factory returning callable function(y_pred, y_true, driver) closure suitable physics_loss_fn temporal_convlstm_fit(). Penalises deviations predicted temporal increments -lambda0 * (y - y_inf) – site-specific rate drawn fitted Pilar 2 ODE rather hard-coded scalar. Dispatches torch-tensor R-array inputs via duck-typing. qf_krr_on_gat_embeddings() (P6 x P10) – composes qf_krr_fit() GAT node embeddings PCA reduction; strict generalisation v2.0.0 foundation-quantum fusion now bakes network structure quantum kernel’s input representation.

title: edaphos 2.7.0
text: New R/pilar9_diffusion_torch.R. Encoder: Conv2d(1 -> base_ch) x2 + pool, Conv2d(base_ch -> 2 base_ch) x2 + pool. Bottleneck: Conv2d(2 base_ch -> 4 base_ch) + sinusoidal time-embedding bias. Decoder skip connections via upsample-nearest + Conv2d + concat. Final 1x1 Conv predict noise eps_theta(x_t, t, c). Classifier-free guidance style: conditioning vector dropped probability 0.1 training model supports unconditional sampling inference. .torch_ddpm_sample – ancestral sampling identical v2.5.0 pure-R formula using autograd-trained U-Net.

title: edaphos 2.7.0

Per-head attention logits a_l^T W h_src + a_r^T W h_dst leaky-ReLU, softmax-normalised per source via index_add scatter. Head outputs concatenated non-final layers, averaged final layer. .torch_gat_module – stack attention layers + linear head. Weight decay 1e-4 Adam optimiser; full backprop every attention weight. three pilares record backend = "r" backend = "torch" fit object; predict / sample methods dispatch accordingly. what: Pilar 10 – Graph Attention Networks on WoSIS co-location graphs
title: edaphos 2.6.0
text: Activates v2.1.0 scaffold. First pedometric application (2026) attention-weighted message-passing soil-profile graphs: profiles nodes, k-NN geographic co-location defines edges inverse-distance weights, Graph Attention Network (Velickovic et al. 2018) propagates covariate information neighbouring sites. gnn_build_graph(profiles, k, feature_cols) – constructs k-NN co-location graph. Returns edaphos_gnn_graph S3 object standardised node features, dense edge_index (n*k rows), soft-normalised edge_weight, spatial coordinates. gnn_fit(graph, targets, hidden, n_heads, n_layers, epochs, lr,   seed) – trains multi-head GAT. layer’s per-head output uses attention rule alpha_ij = softmax_j( LeakyReLU( ’ [Wh_i || Wh_j] ) ) head outputs concatenated layer boundaries. Final node embeddings mapped target linear head ridge regularisation, trained analytic gradient descent (hidden layers fixed random init, ELM-style). gnn_embed(fit) – returns (n, hidden * n_heads) node embedding matrix – directly comparable dimensionality MoCo v2 Pilar 4 embeddings, enables 2026-novel “classical-geospatial vs foundation-model” embedding comparison. predict.edaphos_gnn_gat(fit) – per-node target predictions native target scale (un-standardised). Tests: 6 expectations covering: * Graph schema: nk edges, per-node soft-normalised weights.  Auto-selection numeric feature columns feature_cols =   NULL. * Fit output dimensionality (emb_dim = hidden * n_heads). * Training MSE strictly decreases. * Predictions native scale, length n, positively correlated truth. * Print methods graph fit.

what: Pilar 9 – Denoising-Diffusion Probabilistic Models for soil maps
title: edaphos 2.5.0
text: Activates v2.1.0 scaffold. First pedometric application (2026) DDPMs generative soil mapping: samples ENTIRE plausible maps conditional covariates via iterative denoising (Ho, Jain Abbeel 2020). dm_cosine_schedule(T, s) – Nichol & Dhariwal (2021) cosine schedule. Monotone-decreasing alphabar, clamped [1e-8,   0.9999] numerical stability. dm_fit(stack, conditioning, T, epochs, hidden, lr, seed) – trains tiny 2-D denoiser. 2-hidden-layer MLP sinusoidal time embedding predicts noise eps_theta(x_t, t, c). Training minimises score-matching surrogate loss via analytic gradient descent output layer (hidden layers fixed random initialisation, spirit Huang et al. 2006 ELMs). dm_sample(fit, n_samples, conditioning, seed) – ancestral sampling (Ho et al. 2020, Algorithm 2): starts Gaussian noise t = T walks back t = 0 using posterior mean formula plus learned noise schedule. Conditioning vectors arbitrary dimension supported via conditioning argument – useful soil-map generation gated per-patch covariate summaries (climate zone, land-use class, etc.). Tests: 5 expectations synthetic smoothed random fields: * Cosine schedule monotonicity. * Fit-object schema. * Sample returns right (n_samples, H, W) array. * Conditioning vectors honoured sample time. * Print method. pure-R implementation runs seconds patches 8 x 8. torch port proper 2-D U-Net (16 x 16 / 32 x 32 patches) scheduled v2.5.1.

what: Pilar 8 – Neural operators for pedogenetic depth PDEs
title: edaphos 2.4.0
text: Activates v2.1.0 scaffold. Ships two neural-operator architectures pure R (torch dependency), learning solution map u(z) -> y(z) collection (covariate trajectory, target profile) pairs single trained operator predicts new-site profiles without re-fitting. no_fno_fit(depths, targets, covariates, n_modes, width,   n_blocks, epochs, lr, seed) – 1-D Fourier Neural Operator (Li et al. 2021). Spectral convolutions via stats::fft real magnitude multiplier first n_modes frequencies (numerically stable IFFT round-pure R); pointwise linear residual path; leaky-ReLU activation; trained finite-difference gradient descent output-layer weights. trunk net (query depth -> p-dim) combined inner product. Analytic gradients last two layers; fast enough train 20 x 12 profile problem < 1 s. produce predict() methods aligned new covariate matrix + optional new depths. Tests: 5 expectations covering: * Output-shape equality FNO predict. * Output-shape equality DeepONet predict. * DeepONet training loss strictly decreases. * DeepONet predict new depth grid. * Print methods architectures.

title: edaphos 2.3.0
text: Activates v2.1.0 scaffold full pure-R Gibbs sampler Bayesian spatial linear model inverse-Gamma priors sigma^2 tau^2 Gaussian prior beta. Phi estimated profile-MLE (empirical Bayes) keep every conditional posterior closed-form. "gibbs" (default, external deps): self-contained Gibbs sampler updates beta (multivariate Gaussian), sigma^2 tau^2 (inverse-Gamma), latent spatial field w (sparse precision-form Gaussian). ~2000 iters ~1 s n = 80. "spBayes": dispatches spBayes::spLM (Finley, Banerjee Carlin 2007) Suggests-package installed – full MCMC phi, sigma.sq, tau.sq, beta. predict.edaphos_bhs(object, newdata, quantiles, n_draws) – Bayesian kriging new sites via posterior-draw mean + sd + user- specified quantiles. as_edaphos_posterior.edaphos_bhs(x) – v1.6.0 integration; exposes fitted-value posterior map-type edaphos_posterior. print.edaphos_bhs – posterior summary beta, sigma^2, tau^2. Tests: 5 expectations synthetic spatial dataset (n = 80) – recovery true beta within 3 posterior sds, phi inside user bracket, predict frame schema, edaphos_posterior adapter, print method. spBayes added Suggests.
code: y_i = x_i' beta + w_i + eps_i,     eps_i ~ N(0, tau^2) w   ~ N_n(0, sigma^2 R(phi)),      R_{ij} = exp(-phi d_ij)

what: Pilar 1 x Pilar 3 bridge – Causal 4D (time-varying effects)
title: edaphos 2.2.1
text: Activates v2.1.0 scaffold. Estimates beta_hat(t) sliding window temporal data frame, bootstrap CIs per window non-parametric Mann-Kendall trend test. causal_effect_time_varying(frame, dag, exposure, outcome, window,   step, adjustment, B, min_n, seed) – returns edaphos_causal_4d data frame columns t_start, t_end, t_centre, n, beta_hat, se, ci_lo, ci_hi. Adjustment set auto-derived DAG (Pilar 1 machinery) taken adjustment directly. causal_effect_trend_test(beta_df) – Mann-Kendall S statistic, Kendall tau, normal-approximation p-value, categorical trend_direction {increasing, decreasing, none}. causal_4d_plot(object) – ggplot2 plot beta(t) trajectory CI ribbon trend-test summary subtitle. Tests: 5 expectations covering frame schema, monotonic-increase detection strong linear-rise beta, flat-trend behaviour, error missing columns, ggplot2 return class.

what: Pilar 2 x Pilar 6 bridge – Physics-Informed Quantum Kernels
title: edaphos 2.2.0
text: Activates v2.1.0 scaffold. ZZFeatureMap kernel fused RBF similarity ODE-profile residuals: K_PI(x_i, x_j) = alpha * K_quantum(x_i, x_j) + (1 - alpha) * exp( - (e_i - e_j)^2 / (2 sigma^2) ) e_i = y_i - y_hat_ODE(z_i, x_i) uses fitted Pilar 2 ODE. piml_quantum_kernel(X, y, depths, ode_fit, alpha, sigma, reps,   backend) – PSD alpha [0, 1]; alpha = 1 recovers pure v2.0.0 quantum kernel; alpha = 0 recovers pure physics-residual kernel. Default alpha = 0.7 trusts quantum lift keeping physics regulariser. piml_qkrr_fit(X, y, depths, ode_fit, alpha, sigma, reps, lambda,   backend) – closed-form KRR PI kernel; predict() handles ODE forward step + kernel row + dual sum. Graceful fallback: ode_fit lacks predict() method (e.g. bare list lambda0, mu, y_inf, y0), bridge uses analytic exponential-decay surrogate. Tests: 4 expectations covering PSD alpha {0, 0.3, 0.7, 1}, numerical equality pure quantum kernel alpha = 1, round-trip predict synthetic ODE pedon, print-method sanity.

what: Rcpp port of the quantum-kernel simulator (10-50x speedup)
title: edaphos 2.1.3
text: v2.1.0 roadmap flagged quantum_kernel() O(n^2 * 4^n) pure R. v2.1.3 ships C++ port preserves numerical output machine precision typically runs 10-50x faster. src/quantum_kernel_rcpp.cpp — pure-C++ ZZFeatureMap state- vector simulator -place O(2^n) gate application, pre- computed X / Y state banks, std::complex<double> throughout, std::norm() |.|^2, symmetric-kernel lower-triangle skip. quantum_kernel(X, Y, reps, backend) — new backend argument (default "rcpp", fallback "r" audit Rcpp-less builds). backends produce identical matrices 1e-16. Validation: tests/testthat/test-quantum-kernel-rcpp.R checks agreement n_qubits = 2..5, reps = 1..2, asymmetric (X, Y), PSD invariant (non-negative eigenvalues), measurable speedup. Benchmark (Apple M1 Max, 100 samples, 4 qubits, reps = 2): R backend = 0.049 s; Rcpp backend = 0.004 s; 12.3x speedup. Package now ships src/ LinkingTo: Rcpp. Imports: Rcpp (>= 1.0.0) added. existing pillars work unchanged.

what: Pillar 4 × Pillar 6 — Quantum kernel over foundation embeddings
title: edaphos 2.0.0
text: ambitious bridge package. Foundation-model embeddings (MoCo v2 output, 64 dims) PCA-reduced top-n PCs rescaled [-pi, pi], lifted 2^n-dimensional Hilbert space ZZFeatureMap, kernel ridge regression solved closed form. qf_embed_reduce(embeddings, n_pcs) — PCA + pi-scale. qf_kernel_compare(X_q, reps) — Frobenius distance quantum, RBF, linear Gram matrices feature set. qf_krr_fit(embeddings, y, n_pcs, reps, lambda) — composite fit; predict() handles PCA + pi-rescale + quantum forward pipeline. qf_krr_benchmark(embeddings, covariates, y, ...) — four-way head--head: ranger (raw), RBF-KRR (PCs), Q-KRR (raw), Q-KRR (PCs). Benchmark 1 095 Cerrado profiles (5-fold spatial CV, synthetic stack): four methods tie ~14 g/kg RMSE. ranger reaches R² > 0. infrastructure complete; decisive test awaits encoder v2 (training) + real SoilGrids/WorldClim/SRTM rasters.

title: edaphos 1.9.2
text: Complements Sargan exclusion test partial-R² robustness diagnostic: strong latent confounder need zero estimated effect? causal_sensitivity_summary(effect, se, df, q, alpha) — Robustness Value (RV) RV_alpha verbal interpretation. Validated published Theorem 4.4 example (RV = 0.1810 recovered four decimal places). causal_sensitivity_grid() — 2-D bias-adjustment grid contour plotting. causal_sensitivity_from_lm() / causal_sensitivity_from_iv() — convenience wrappers lm edaphos_causal_iv fits. Applied 1 095 Cerrado profiles: robust causal claim Clay → SOC naive OLS (RV = 24%). MAP → SOC backdoor OLS RV = 8%, dropping 4% real-encoder IV due weak instruments synthetic-stack mode.

Setting localization_radius = 2 v1.5.0 runner recovers 3.2 % net RMSE reduction.

what: Honest repair of the Cerrado benchmark
title: edaphos 1.3.1
text: v1.3.0 shipped first real-data benchmark four load-bearing flaws produced unhelpfully low R² misleading “E worse B1” story. v1.3.1 fixes : Clean, physically comparable topsoil target. filter changed “horizon lower_depth <= 30” (mixed 0–5 cm, 5–15 cm 15–30 cm horizons) shallowest horizon starts upper_depth == 0 lower_depth 5–30 cm. Every profile now contributes one physically comparable SOC concentration measurement g/kg. Relaxed positional_uncertainty ≤ 2 km matches 1 km covariate resolution. 1095 profiles survive filters — 3.6× gain v1.3.0’s 302-profile strict cut. full integrated 0–30 cm SOC stock target attempted abandoned: WoSIS’s per- horizon bulk density covers ~20 % Brazilian profiles stock formulation degenerates constant-BD-fallback target weaker signal plain concentration. Land cover bioclim covariates added. ESA WorldCover 2020 fractional covers (6 layers: trees / grassland / shrubs / cropland / built / bare) @Zanaga2021worldcover (CC--4.0) plus 19 WorldClim 2.1 bioclim indices @Fick2017worldclim. Total covariate count grew 32 56. 5-fold spatial cross-validation (k-means coordinates) replaces single 80/20 split. Every profile test point exactly ; 302 pooled predictions give binomial CIs ~4× tighter 60-point test set v1.3.0 eliminate +6 g/kg bias floor came unlucky train/test SOC distribution drift. log transform. log1p target hurts data (R² 0.16 log vs 0.23 linear); clean 0–10 cm distribution skewed enough benefit variance stabilisation. Target raw SOC g/kg, point estimate bagged-tree mean, interval 2.5/97.5 QRF quantiles.

previous_headings: Honest repair of the Cerrado benchmark
title: edaphos 1.3.1
text: R² 0.22–0.23 line published Brazilian Cerrado / savanna DSM (Gomes et al. 2019: 0.13 Brazil-wide; Nakhavali et al. 2018: 0.28 savanna 200 profiles). plain QRF calibration champion (PICP 0.944, best interval score 65.8); residual kriging gains +0.014 R² ruins calibration (PICP falls 0.82). foundation-model embedding (v1 encoder, 20 000 InfoNCE steps) still trails B1. encoder v2 200 000 steps (10× v1 budget) currently training; v1.3.2 re-runs benchmark v2 weights separate Zenodo deposit.

what: Honest benchmark on real Brazilian Cerrado data
title: edaphos 1.3.0
text: New vignette case-cerrado-end--end: end--end reproducible case study 1212 real WoSIS topsoil profiles (Batjes et al. 2020) across full Cerrado biome (IBGE polygon via geobr), held-240-profile test set stratified 2×2 longitude × latitude quadrants. Three competing stacks split, covariates seeds: B1 ranger quantile regression forest 32-layer SoilGrids + WorldClim + SRTM covariate stack; B2 B1 + gstat residual kriging (Hengl-style classical DSM); E B1 + 64-dim edaphos-cerrado-moco-v1 MoCo v2 foundation embedding (Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19701276). Honest result: Cerrado benchmark foundation-model embedding improve raw-covariate ranger (RMSE 12.53 vs 12.28 g/kg). calibration champion plain QRF (PICP = 0.946 0.95 nominal level, interval score 57.5). README updated accordingly — “beyond state art” claim explicitly scoped regimes raw covariate stack thinner SoilGrids + WorldClim + SRTM. New helper R/edaphos_metrics.R: edaphos_rmse(), edaphos_mae(), edaphos_r2(), edaphos_bias(), edaphos_picp(), edaphos_interval_score(), edaphos_ece(), edaphos_metrics_summary() — single namespace every benchmark package. 21 new unit tests.
